commit.message:
feat(ios): redesign AgentsView, add agent update flow (#1469)

## Summary

Redesigns AgentsView (the agent catalog) to match the Zoo Square v2
EXPLORE design and adds a new agent-update affordance plus a global
"agent hired" toast that survives tab switches.

### Key changes
- **EXPLORE-style row layout**: gradient background, HankenGrotesk
typography, dark navy active pill, dynamic category tabs driven by the
catalog response
- **Liquid glass menu button** (iOS 26) — fixed a latent `#if
swift(>=6.2)` gate that was preprocessing the effect out under
`SWIFT_VERSION=5.0`
- **API-driven categories**: removed the hardcoded `AgentCategory` enum;
`selectedCategory: String?` filters live against catalog `category`
values, deduped case-insensitively
- **Reusable `Toast` component** (`Views/Components/Toast.swift`) with
optional avatar/icon/emoji + action button, plus a
`.toast(item:autoDismissAfter:)` view modifier
- **Agent update flow**: when `UserAgent.has_update == true`, row shows
`plus.arrow.trianglehead.clock` + a "NEW" pill; tap calls `POST
/openclaw/agents/{id}/redeploy` (synchronous), refreshes user agents
- **Hired toast is now global** — lives on `AppShellView` (Layer 4 in
the shell ZStack) so it persists across tab switches; "Say hi" navigates
to chat
- **Hired-action simplification**: ellipsis sheet (Chat Now / Fire /
Cancel) replaced with a direct `ellipsis.message`-tap that opens chat
- **Centralized colors**: promoted `deepNavy` (#0B0F1A), `accentRed`
(#E63946), `coolTextSecondary` (#5A6478), `coolBorderLight` (#E8EBF0) to
top-level AppTheme; collapsed 5+ duplicate hex literals across
PaywallView, VoiceWaveformView, RedeemGiftCodeView, ComposeInputPanel
- **Model alignment with backend**: removed `AgentCatalogItem.isNew`
(backend never produced `is_new`); removed `UserAgent.isDefault`
(backend never produced it either; SidebarDrawerView now distinguishes
the main agent by `id == "main"`)

### Backend touchpoints (already shipped)
- `POST /openclaw/agents/{agent_id}/redeploy` — used by the new update
flow
- `GET /openclaw/agents` — `has_update` field per agent (computed in
`services/openclaw/agent_response.py`)

## Test plan
- [ ] Open AgentsView — verify EXPLORE layout, gradient background,
category tabs populated from catalog
- [ ] Liquid glass menu button visible at top-left and refracts content
beneath
- [ ] Tap `+` on an unhired agent — verify hire completes, "Hired!"
toast slides in from the top with avatar + "Say hi"
- [ ] Switch to chat tab while toast is on screen — toast persists
- [ ] Tap "Say hi" — navigates to chat with that agent
- [ ] Wait 5s without action — toast auto-dismisses
- [ ] Hire a second agent rapidly — second toast replaces the first with
a fresh 5s timer
- [ ] For an agent with `has_update == true` — NEW pill on title and
update icon on the right; tap shows spinner during redeploy and clears
`has_update` after refresh
- [ ] For a hired agent — tap `ellipsis.message` icon — opens chat
directly (no sheet)
- [ ] Filter by category — verify only matching agents show; tap "All"
pill returns full catalog
- [ ] `xcodebuild test -only-testing:ZooClawTests/AgentServiceTests
-only-testing:ZooClawTests/AgentViewModelTests` passes
- [ ] `swiftlint --strict` — 0 violations

## Notes
- 11 commits, each independently buildable. Linear history, fully
rebased on latest `main`.
- Backend has no per-agent `update` endpoint; we use the existing
`redeploy` which atomically pulls the latest catalog version.
Synchronous on the server, so iOS doesn't need polling.
- The new `Toast` component is generic — `RedeemSuccessToast` /
`ModelDegradationToast` could migrate to it in a follow-up if desired.

+import SwiftUI
+
+/// Bottom-anchored action sheet with a destructive "Fire" option + Cancel.
+///
+/// Designed to be presented via `.sheet(isPresented:)` with a fixed-height
+/// `presentationDetent`. Tapping "Fire" here does NOT actually fire the
+/// agent — it signals intent via `onFire`, then dismisses, so the parent
+/// can chain into a follow-up `FireConfirmationDialog` (two-stage
+/// destructive guard).
+struct AgentMoreActionSheet: View {
+    let onFire: () -> Void
+    @Environment(\.dismiss) private var dismiss
+
+    var body: some View {
+        VStack(spacing: 10) {
+            fireCard
+            cancelCard
+        }
+        .padding(.top, 16)
+        .padding(.horizontal, 30)
+    }
+
+    // MARK: - Subviews
+
+    private var fireCard: some View {
+        Button {
+            onFire()
+            dismiss()
+        } label: {
+            Text("Fire")
+                .font(.system(size: 16, weight: .medium))
+                .foregroundStyle(AppTheme.dangerBright)
+                .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
+                .padding(.vertical, 14)
+                .background(Color.white)
+                .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 14))
+        }
+        .buttonStyle(.plain)
+    }
+
+    private var cancelCard: some View {
+        Button {
+            dismiss()
+        } label: {
+            Text("Cancel")
+                .font(.system(size: 16))
+                .foregroundStyle(AppTheme.textPrimary)
+                .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
+                .padding(.vertical, 14)
+                .background(Color(red: 0.93, green: 0.93, blue: 0.94))
+                .cornerRadius(1000)
+        }
+        .buttonStyle(.plain)
+    }
+}

+import SwiftUI
+
+/// A reusable top-of-screen toast with optional avatar and action button.
+///
+/// Drive presentation with the `.toast(item:autoDismissAfter:content:)` modifier:
+///
+/// ```swift
+/// .toast(item: $hiredAgent, autoDismissAfter: 5) { agent in
+///     Toast(
+///         leading: .avatarURL(URL(string: agent.avatarURL), fallback: agent.emoji),
+///         title: "\(agent.name) Hired!",
+///         subtitle: "\(agent.name) has joined your team.",
+///         action: .init(label: "Say hi") {
+///             hiredAgent = nil
+///             onChatNow?(agent)
+///         }
+///     )
+/// }
+/// ```
+struct Toast: View {
+    enum Leading {
+        case avatarURL(URL?, fallback: String)
+        /// Avatar with a small SF Symbol badge overlapping the bottom-trailing
+        /// edge — e.g. `minus.circle.fill` red for "removed", or
+        /// `checkmark.circle.fill` green for "added".
+        case avatarURLWithBadge(URL?, fallback: String, badgeIcon: String, badgeTint: Color)
+        case icon(name: String, tint: Color)
+        case emoji(String)
+        case none
+    }
+
+    struct Action {
+        let label: String
+        let handler: () -> Void
+    }
+
+    let leading: Leading
+    let title: String
+    let subtitle: String?
+    let action: Action?
+    /// When non-nil, renders a trailing "✕" button that invokes this handler.
+    /// Independent of `action`; both can coexist if a toast needs an action
+    /// and an explicit dismiss affordance.
+    let onClose: (() -> Void)?
+
+    init(
+        leading: Leading = .none,
+        title: String,
+        subtitle: String? = nil,
+        action: Action? = nil,
+        onClose: (() -> Void)? = nil
+    ) {
+        self.leading = leading
+        self.title = title
+        self.subtitle = subtitle
+        self.action = action
+        self.onClose = onClose
+    }
+
+    var body: some View {
+        HStack(spacing: 12) {
+            leadingView
+
+            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
+                Text(title)
+                    .font(.custom("HankenGrotesk-Bold", size: 15).weight(.bold))
+                    .foregroundStyle(AppTheme.deepNavy)
+                    .lineLimit(1)
+                if let subtitle, !subtitle.isEmpty {
+                    Text(subtitle)
+                        .font(.custom("HankenGrotesk-Regular", size: 13))
+                        .foregroundStyle(AppTheme.coolTextSecondary)
+                        .lineLimit(2)
+                }
+            }
+            .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .leading)
+
+            if let action {
+                Button(action: action.handler) {
+                    Text(action.label)
+                        .font(.custom("HankenGrotesk-Medium", size: 14).weight(.medium))
+                        .foregroundStyle(.white)
+                        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
+                        .padding(.vertical, 10)
+                        .background(AppTheme.deepNavy)
+                        .clipShape(Capsule())
+                }
+                .buttonStyle(.plain)
+            }
+
+            if let onClose {
+                Button(action: onClose) {
+                    Image(systemName: "xmark")
+                        .font(.system(size: 16, weight: .medium))
+                        .foregroundStyle(AppTheme.coolTextSecondary)
+                        .frame(width: 32, height: 32)
+                }
+                .buttonStyle(.plain)
+            }
+        }
+        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
+        .padding(.vertical, 12)
+        .background(.white)
+        .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 12))
+        .overlay(
+            RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 12)
+                .stroke(Color.black.opacity(0.12), lineWidth: 1)
+        )
+        .shadow(color: .black.opacity(0.1), radius: 6, x: 0, y: 6)
+    }
+
+    @ViewBuilder
+    private var leadingView: some View {
+        switch leading {
+        case .avatarURL(let url, let fallback):
+            avatarCircle(url: url, fallback: fallback)
+
+        case .avatarURLWithBadge(let url, let fallback, let badgeIcon, let badgeTint):
+            avatarCircle(url: url, fallback: fallback)
+                .overlay(alignment: .bottomTrailing) {
+                    Image(systemName: badgeIcon)
+                        .font(.system(size: 18))
+                        .foregroundStyle(badgeTint, .white)
+                        .symbolRenderingMode(.palette)
+                        .background(Circle().fill(.white))
+                        .offset(x: 2, y: 2)
+                }
+
+        case .icon(let name, let tint):
+            Image(systemName: name)
+                .font(.system(size: 22))
+                .foregroundStyle(tint)
+                .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
+
+        case .emoji(let emoji):
+            Text(emoji)
+                .font(.system(size: 28))
+                .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
+
+        case .none:
+            EmptyView()
+        }
+    }
+
+    private func avatarCircle(url: URL?, fallback: String) -> some View {
+        Group {
+            if let url {
+                CachedAsyncImage(url: url) {
+                    Text(fallback).font(.system(size: 22))
+                }
+            } else {
+                Text(fallback)
+                    .font(.system(size: 22))
+                    .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, maxHeight: .infinity)
+                    .background(AppTheme.bgInput)
+            }
+        }
+        .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
+        .clipShape(Circle())
+    }
+}
+
+// MARK: - Presentation modifier
+
+extension View {
+    /// Show a `Toast` from the top of the screen, driven by an optional binding.
+    ///
+    /// The toast renders while `item` is non-nil. Setting `autoDismissAfter` to a
+    /// non-nil number schedules an automatic dismissal (sets `item = nil` after
+    /// that many seconds). Pass `nil` to require manual dismissal — useful when
+    /// the toast carries an action the user must read before deciding.
+    func toast<Item>(
+        item: Binding<Item?>,
+        autoDismissAfter seconds: TimeInterval? = nil,
+        @ViewBuilder content: @escaping (Item) -> Toast
+    ) -> some View {
+        modifier(ToastItemModifier(item: item, autoDismissAfter: seconds, toastContent: content))
+    }
+}
+
+private struct ToastItemModifier<Item>: ViewModifier {
+    @Binding var item: Item?
+    let autoDismissAfter: TimeInterval?
+    let toastContent: (Item) -> Toast
+
+    func body(content: Content) -> some View {
+        ZStack(alignment: .top) {
+            content
+            if let value = item {
+                toastContent(value)
+                    .padding(.horizontal, 16)
+                    .padding(.top, 12)
+                    .transition(.move(edge: .top).combined(with: .opacity))
+                    .task {
+                        guard let seconds = autoDismissAfter else { return }
+                        try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(seconds))
+                        if !Task.isCancelled {
+                            withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.3)) {
+                                item = nil
+                            }
+                        }
+                    }
+            }
+        }
+        .animation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.3), value: item != nil)
+    }
+}

+import Foundation
+import Testing
+@testable import ZooClaw
+
+/// Covers `AgentViewModel.updateAgent` (redeploy) flow: success refresh,
+/// optimistic local clear when post-refresh fails, and failure preservation
+/// of `userAgents` when redeploy itself fails. Split from `AgentViewModelTests`
+/// so the original suite stays under SwiftLint's `type_body_length` cap.
+@MainActor
+@Suite(.serialized) struct AgentViewModelUpdateTests {
+
+    private static let testHost = "agent-vm-update-test.example.com"
+
+    private func makeAgentService(
+        handler: @Sendable @escaping (URLRequest) throws -> (HTTPURLResponse, Data)
+    ) -> AgentService {
+        let config = URLSessionConfiguration.ephemeral
+        config.protocolClasses = [MockURLProtocol.self]
+        MockURLProtocol.setHandler(forHost: Self.testHost, handler: handler)
+        let session = URLSession(configuration: config)
+        let networkService = NetworkService(
+            tokenProvider: MockTokenProvider(),
+            baseURL: URL(string: "https://\(Self.testHost)")!,
+            session: session
+        )
+        return AgentService(networkService: networkService)
+    }
+
+    nonisolated private func userAgentsResponse(
+        _ request: URLRequest,
+        hasUpdate: Bool,
+        statusCode: Int = 200
+    ) throws -> (HTTPURLResponse, Data) {
+        let response = HTTPURLResponse(
+            url: request.url!, statusCode: statusCode, httpVersion: nil, headerFields: nil
+        )!
+        if statusCode != 200 {
+            return (response, Data())
+        }
+        let payload: [String: Any] = [
+            "agents": [
+                ["id": "research", "name": "Researcher", "emoji": "🔬", "has_update": hasUpdate]
+            ]
+        ]
+        return (response, try JSONSerialization.data(withJSONObject: payload))
+    }
+
+    nonisolated private func redeployOkResponse(_ request: URLRequest) throws -> (HTTPURLResponse, Data) {
+        let response = HTTPURLResponse(
+            url: request.url!, statusCode: 200, httpVersion: nil, headerFields: nil
+        )!
+        let ok: [String: Any] = ["ok": true, "agent_id": "research"]
+        return (response, try JSONSerialization.data(withJSONObject: ok))
+    }
+
+    @Test func updateAgentClearsHasUpdateAndRefreshesAgents() async throws {
+        var firstFetch = true
+        let service = makeAgentService { request in
+            let path = request.url?.path ?? ""
+            if path.hasSuffix("/redeploy") {
+                return try self.redeployOkResponse(request)
+            }
+            let hasUpdate = firstFetch
+            firstFetch = false
+            return try self.userAgentsResponse(request, hasUpdate: hasUpdate)
+        }
+        let viewModel = AgentViewModel(agentService: service)
+        await viewModel.refreshUserAgents()
+        #expect(viewModel.userAgents.first?.hasUpdate == true)
+
+        let success = await viewModel.updateAgent(id: "research")
+
+        #expect(success == true)
+        #expect(viewModel.userAgents.first?.hasUpdate == false)
+    }
+
+    @Test func updateAgentClearsHasUpdateOptimisticallyEvenIfRefreshFails() async throws {
+        var redeployDone = false
+        let service = makeAgentService { request in
+            let path = request.url?.path ?? ""
+            if path.hasSuffix("/redeploy") {
+                redeployDone = true
+                return try self.redeployOkResponse(request)
+            }
+            if redeployDone {
+                return try self.userAgentsResponse(request, hasUpdate: true, statusCode: 500)
+            }
+            return try self.userAgentsResponse(request, hasUpdate: true)
+        }
+        let viewModel = AgentViewModel(agentService: service)
+        await viewModel.refreshUserAgents()
+        #expect(viewModel.userAgents.first?.hasUpdate == true)
+
+        let success = await viewModel.updateAgent(id: "research")
+
+        #expect(success == true)
+        #expect(viewModel.userAgents.first?.hasUpdate == false)
+    }
+
+    @Test func updateAgentReturnsFalseAndPreservesUserAgentsOnRedeployFailure() async throws {
+        let service = makeAgentService { request in
+            let path = request.url?.path ?? ""
+            if path.hasSuffix("/redeploy") {
+                return try self.userAgentsResponse(request, hasUpdate: true, statusCode: 500)
+            }
+            return try self.userAgentsResponse(request, hasUpdate: true)
+        }
+        let viewModel = AgentViewModel(agentService: service)
+        await viewModel.refreshUserAgents()
+
+        let success = await viewModel.updateAgent(id: "research")
+
+        #expect(success == false)
+        #expect(viewModel.userAgents.first?.hasUpdate == true)
+    }
+}

commit.message:
test(web): migrate inline queryBy*().toBeNull() to jest-dom (#1046) (#1478)

## Summary
Follow-up to #1029 jest-dom adoption. Two parts:

1. **Migrate 78 inline RTL presence assertions across 15 files**
- 46 sites in 11 `tests/unit/app/` files (the `app/` batch noted in
#1046)
- 32 sites in 4 `tests/unit/components/` files that #1029's
`.toBeDefined()` migration script didn't touch (it migrated the positive
form but not `.toBeNull()` / `.not.toBeNull()` on `queryBy*`)
   - Transforms (mirror of #1029):
- `expect((screen.)?queryBy*(...)).toBeNull()` →
`.not.toBeInTheDocument()`
- `expect((screen.)?queryBy*(...)).not.toBeNull()` →
`.toBeInTheDocument()`
- `expect(screen.getByText(...)).not.toBeNull()` →
`.toBeInTheDocument()` (one site in `agents-manager-publish`)
- Variable-reference forms (~91 sites in `app/`, getAttribute on local
consts) remain out of scope — they need per-site type tracing.

2. **Add symmetric `no-restricted-syntax` lint guards for
`tests/unit/**`** mirroring #1029's `.toBeDefined()` rule:
   - bans `expect(queryBy*(...)).toBeNull()`
   - bans `expect(queryBy*(...)).not.toBeNull()`
The rule's existing-violation list was the 32 sites in `components/`
cleaned up in part 1.

## Out of scope
- `tests/unit/hooks/` — survey found 0 zero-ambiguity inline candidates
(the issue's "biggest payoff" call was based on `render`/import counts,
not on actual assertion patterns; hook tests assert on
`result.current.*`, not DOM nodes). No PR worth doing.
- Variable-reference forms (e.g. `const el = getByTestId(...);
expect(el).not.toBeNull()`) — per-site type tracing, separate PR.
- Hygiene items still on #1046: `pnpm.overrides` location,
`@xyflow/system` transitive vitest@1.6.1 dedup.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm lint` green (cache clean — both new lint selectors pass
after part 1)
- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` green
- [x] `pnpm test:unit` on all 15 affected files → 364 passed / 1 todo, 0
new failures
- [ ] CI `code-quality / lint-and-test` green

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- `tests/unit/hooks/` — survey found 0 zero-ambiguity inline candidates
(the issue's "biggest payoff" call was based on `render`/import counts,
not on actual assertion patterns; hook tests assert on
`result.current.*`, not DOM nodes). No PR worth doing.
- Variable-reference forms (e.g. `const el = getByTestId(...);
expect(el).not.toBeNull()`) — per-site type tracing, separate PR.
- Hygiene items still on #1046: `pnpm.overrides` location,
`@xyflow/system` transitive vitest@1.6.1 dedup.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm lint` green (cache clean — both new lint selectors pass
after part 1)
- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` green
- [x] `pnpm test:unit` on all 15 affected files → 364 passed / 1 todo, 0
new failures
- [ ] CI `code-quality / lint-and-test` green

commit.message:
fix(web): sanitize video URL in <video> attribute (#1328) (#1462)

## Summary

`lib/api/chat.ts` 的 `sendVideoGeneration` 之前把 `videoItem.url` 原样塞进
template literal 的 \`<video controls src=\"\${url}\">\`。如果 URL 出现
\`\"\`,attribute 边界被打破,后面的内容会被 HTML 解析成相邻属性(如 \`onload=\"alert(1)\"\`)——
而这串内容是会被存进 chat session 然后回放成 HTML 的。\`b64_json\` 分支由 data-URL 前缀和
base64 字符集约束,实际暴露面是 URL 分支。

抽 \`buildVideoResponseContentHtml(src)\`,改用
\`document.createElement('video')\` + \`outerHTML\` —— 让浏览器 HTML
serializer 按 WHATWG 规范处理 attribute escaping(\`&\` → \`&\`, \`\"\` →
\`"\`)。一个含 \`\"\` 的 URL 在序列化→重解析后会**圆环回到原值**,不会冒出新属性。

## Test

新建 \`tests/unit/lib/api/chat.unit.spec.ts\`,3 个用例:

- 安全 URL 输出 baseline 形式(controls + src + style)
- \`data:video/mp4;base64,...\` 原样保留
- **回归断言**(#1328):一组带 \`\"\` / \`&\` / \`<\` / 拼接 \`onerror=...\` 的
tricky URL,断言 \`getAttribute('src')\` 与原 URL 完全相等,且 attribute 名集合恒为
\`['controls', 'src', 'style']\`(无注入)

本地反向验证:把 helper 临时改回 template-literal 形式,回归用例报 \`expected
'https://x.com/a' to be 'https://x.com/a\"b.mp4'\` —— 测试确实拦得住
regression。

## Closes

Closes #1328

## Test plan

- [x] \`pnpm test:unit tests/unit/lib/api/chat.unit.spec.ts\` (3/3)
- [x] \`pnpm test:unit tests/unit/hooks/useLiteLLMApi.unit.spec.ts\` (现有
caller 不受影响)
- [x] \`npx tsc --noEmit\` clean
- [x] \`npx eslint\` / \`npx prettier --check\` clean
- [ ] CI: code-quality / lint-and-test

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+// @vitest-environment jsdom
+import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'
+
+import { buildVideoResponseContentHtml } from '@/lib/api/chat'
+
+describe('buildVideoResponseContentHtml', () => {
+  it('emits a baseline <video> with controls, src, and style for a safe URL', () => {
+    const html = buildVideoResponseContentHtml('https://artifacts.example.com/clip.mp4')
+    expect(html.startsWith('<video')).toBe(true)
+    expect(html.endsWith('</video>')).toBe(true)
+    expect(html).toContain('controls')
+    expect(html).toContain('src="https://artifacts.example.com/clip.mp4"')
+    expect(html).toContain('max-width: 100%')
+    expect(html).toContain('border-radius: 8px')
+  })
+
+  it('passes data:video/mp4 base64 src through intact', () => {
+    const html = buildVideoResponseContentHtml('data:video/mp4;base64,AAAA')
+    expect(html).toContain('src="data:video/mp4;base64,AAAA"')
+  })
+
+  // Regression for #1328: signed URLs (with `&` querystring separators) must
+  // round-trip byte-for-byte through the persisted content because downstream
+  // consumers (canvas useCanvasState/useCanvasChat extractVideos regex,
+  // useAssets src= scan, ermp/utils media regex) extract the URL by string
+  // scanning and would re-fetch with a corrupted URL if `&` were HTML-encoded.
+  it('preserves real signed URLs byte-for-byte through the same regex downstream consumers use', () => {
+    const realUrls = [
+      'https://artifacts.example.com/v.mp4?token=abc&exp=123',
+      'https://s3.amazonaws.com/bucket/key?X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&X-Amz-Date=20260429T060000Z&X-Amz-Expires=3600&X-Amz-Signature=deadbeef',
+      'https://litellm-proxy.example.com/videos/abc?key=foo&copy=true',
+    ]
+    for (const src of realUrls) {
+      const html = buildVideoResponseContentHtml(src)
+      const extracted = /<video[^>]+src=["']([^"']+)["'][^>]*>/.exec(html)?.[1]
+      expect(extracted).toBe(src)
+    }
+  })
+
+  // The injection vector from #1328: a `"` (or `<` / `>`) in the URL would
+  // break the WHATWG quoted-attribute boundary and let injected adjacent
+  // attributes (`onload="alert(1)"`) become live HTML on replay. We can't
+  // HTML-encode the URL (it would break the round-trip above), so we reject
+  // those characters as a contract violation. Real BFF-supplied presigned
+  // URLs never contain them.
+  it('throws when src contains attribute-breaking characters', () => {
+    const unsafeUrls = [
+      'https://x.com/a"b.mp4',
+      'https://x.com/a<b.mp4',
+      'https://x.com/a>b.mp4',
+      'https://x.com/path?q=1"onerror="alert(1)',
+    ]
+    for (const src of unsafeUrls) {
+      expect(() => buildVideoResponseContentHtml(src)).toThrow(/HTML-attribute-breaking/)
+    }
+  })
+})

commit.message:
fix(openclaw): wait for async agent operations (#1475)

## Summary
- switch incremental hire and fire flows to the async install and
uninstall endpoints
- wait for agent operations to finish before refreshing frontend agent
state
- centralize async operation polling and failure handling in the
OpenClaw API client

commit.message:
chore: remove unused STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY (#1474)

## Summary
- Remove `STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` from backend settings, `.env.example`,
and setup docs
- This env var was defined but never referenced in any code path
(frontend or backend)
- Publishable keys (`pk_`) are public by design and don't need
server-side config
- Part of a key-leak audit; `pydantic-settings` `extra="ignore"` means
no env cleanup needed

## Test plan
- [ ] CI passes (no code references this field)
- [ ] Backend starts without `STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` in env (already
the case — field had empty default)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(ios): Post-auth onboarding view redesigns (PR-B of 2) (#1467)

## Summary

**PR-B of 2** in the onboarding redesign split. Companion to #1458
(PR-A, **merged**). Ships the Figma redesigns of the four post-auth
views.

After this PR lands, the visual onboarding refresh is feature-complete:
hero → name → role → useCase → register → OTP → agentSelect →
notifications → setupLoading all use the new design.

### EmailOTPLoginView
- 6-box OTP grid with invisible `TextField` driving iOS one-time-code
autofill from SMS / Mail.
- Resend button reads the email back from `authViewModel.emailOTPState`
(fix for the bug PR-A's review caught — previously sent an empty
string).
- `HankenGrotesk-Bold` title, `Inter-Regular` subtitle/countdown.

### AgentSelectView
- Replaces the carousel with a category-tab + scrollable list.
- Multi-select; honors `OnboardingViewModel.maxSelectedAgents = 3` cap
(enforced in-view).
- Surfaces `agentViewModel.catalogError` as a "Couldn't load agents /
Retry" UI when the catalog fetch fails — no infinite spinner on network
blips.
- "Not right now" secondary action sets `selectedAgentIds = []` and
advances to notifications.

### SetupLoadingView
- Consumes `AgentViewModel.installAgent`'s `Bool` return; failed
installs are accumulated into `installFailures` and surfaced via
`errorContent` with a Retry. Removes the silent-failure path where users
were sent to the chat with broken agents.
- Cancels the install `Task` on disappear to prevent zombie polls.

### OnboardingFlowView
- Refactored to the gradient-background + arrow-back-button design.
- Routes the redesigned post-auth views with their updated init
signatures (`EmailOTPLoginView` now takes `viewModel:` alongside
`onAuthenticated:`).

## Test plan

- [ ] Onboarding end-to-end: hero → name → role → useCase → register →
OTP → agentSelect → notifications → setupLoading
- [ ] OTP autofill from SMS / Mail when verification code arrives
- [ ] Resend OTP uses the previously-entered email (not blank)
- [ ] AgentSelect catalog error path: kill network mid-load → "Couldn't
load agents / Retry" appears; retry succeeds when network returns
- [ ] AgentSelect multi-select: cap at 3
- [ ] SetupLoading install failure path: simulate a failed install →
"Couldn't install: <name>" with Retry button; Retry re-runs only failed
installs

## Notes

- Size: **873 / 2000 lines** — comfortable budget.
- Depends on #1458 (merged).
- Closes the onboarding redesign initiative — no further view PRs
anticipated.

notifications → setupLoading all use the new design.

### EmailOTPLoginView
- 6-box OTP grid with invisible `TextField` driving iOS one-time-code
autofill from SMS / Mail.
- Resend button reads the email back from `authViewModel.emailOTPState`
(fix for the bug PR-A's review caught — previously sent an empty
string).
- `HankenGrotesk-Bold` title, `Inter-Regular` subtitle/countdown.

### AgentSelectView
- Replaces the carousel with a category-tab + scrollable list.
- Multi-select; honors `OnboardingViewModel.maxSelectedAgents = 3` cap
(enforced in-view).
- Surfaces `agentViewModel.catalogError` as a "Couldn't load agents /
Retry" UI when the catalog fetch fails — no infinite spinner on network
blips.
- "Not right now" secondary action sets `selectedAgentIds = []` and
advances to notifications.

### SetupLoadingView
- Consumes `AgentViewModel.installAgent`'s `Bool` return; failed
installs are accumulated into `installFailures` and surfaced via
`errorContent` with a Retry. Removes the silent-failure path where users
were sent to the chat with broken agents.
- Cancels the install `Task` on disappear to prevent zombie polls.

### OnboardingFlowView
- Refactored to the gradient-background + arrow-back-button design.
- Routes the redesigned post-auth views with their updated init
signatures (`EmailOTPLoginView` now takes `viewModel:` alongside
`onAuthenticated:`).

## Test plan

- [ ] Onboarding end-to-end: hero → name → role → useCase → register →
OTP → agentSelect → notifications → setupLoading
- [ ] OTP autofill from SMS / Mail when verification code arrives
- [ ] Resend OTP uses the previously-entered email (not blank)
- [ ] AgentSelect catalog error path: kill network mid-load → "Couldn't
load agents / Retry" appears; retry succeeds when network returns
- [ ] AgentSelect multi-select: cap at 3
- [ ] SetupLoading install failure path: simulate a failed install →
"Couldn't install: <name>" with Retry button; Retry re-runs only failed
installs

## Notes

- Size: **873 / 2000 lines** — comfortable budget.
- Depends on #1458 (merged).
- Closes the onboarding redesign initiative — no further view PRs
anticipated.

+import Testing
+@testable import ZooClaw
+
+/// Pins the typed tab-key contract that distinguishes synthesized
+/// "uncategorized" from any backend category named "Other". Without
+/// this, a real category named "Other" would (1) appear duplicated in
+/// the tab bar when uncategorized agents also exist, and (2) silently
+/// hide its own agents because the filter would route to the
+/// uncategorized branch.
+struct AgentSelectViewTabKeyTests {
+
+    @Test func uncategorizedDisplayNameIsOther() {
+        #expect(AgentSelectView.TabKey.uncategorized.displayName == "Other")
+    }
+
+    @Test func categoryDisplayNameIsCapitalized() {
+        #expect(AgentSelectView.TabKey.category("research").displayName == "Research")
+        #expect(AgentSelectView.TabKey.category("creative writing").displayName == "Creative Writing")
+    }
+
+    @Test func realOtherCategoryIsDistinctFromUncategorized() {
+        // The collision check: a category literally named "Other" must
+        // hash and compare differently from .uncategorized.
+        let real = AgentSelectView.TabKey.category("Other")
+        let synthesized = AgentSelectView.TabKey.uncategorized
+        #expect(real != synthesized)
+    }
+
+    @Test func realOtherCategoryDisplayNameStillUsesCapitalizedSource() {
+        // Both render as "Other" visually (intentional — the synthesized
+        // bucket's label and a real "Other" category coincide), but the
+        // KEY identity differs so filteredAgents routes to the right set.
+        let real = AgentSelectView.TabKey.category("Other")
+        #expect(real.displayName == "Other")
+        #expect(AgentSelectView.TabKey.uncategorized.displayName == "Other")
+        #expect(real != AgentSelectView.TabKey.uncategorized)
+    }
+}

     // MARK: - Tests
 
+    // MARK: - installAgents (multi-agent batch)
+
+    @Test func installAgentsEmptySetReturnsEmptyWithoutNetworkCalls() async {
+        // Empty selection (the "Not right now" path) must not hit the
+        // network at all and must return cleanly.
+        var calls = 0
+        let viewModel = makeViewModel { request in
+            calls += 1
+            return Self.errorResponse(request)
+        }
+
+        let failures = await viewModel.installAgents([])
+
+        #expect(failures.isEmpty)
+        #expect(calls == 0)
+    }
+
+    @Test func installAgentsAllSuccessReturnsEmptyFailures() async throws {
+        let createPayload = try operationCreateJSON()
+        let completedPayload = try operationStatusJSON(status: "completed")
+        let agentsPayload = try userAgentsJSON()
+
+        let viewModel = makeViewModel { request in
+            let path = request.url?.path ?? ""
+            if path.contains("/install") {
+                return Self.okResponse(request, createPayload)
+            }
+            if path.contains("/operations/") {
+                return Self.okResponse(request, completedPayload)
+            }
+            return Self.okResponse(request, agentsPayload)
+        }
+
+        let failures = await viewModel.installAgents(["agent1", "agent2", "agent3"])
+
+        #expect(failures.isEmpty)
+    }
+
+    @Test func installAgentsSkipsAgentsAlreadyInUserAgents() async {
+        // Retry path safety: if userAgents already contains the ID (e.g.,
+        // the agent succeeded on a prior attempt and the user tapped Retry
+        // on a partial-failure UI), installAgents must NOT re-install it.
+        // Mock fails every network call so we'd see failures if anything
+        // were attempted.
+        var calls = 0
+        let viewModel = makeViewModel { request in
+            calls += 1
+            return Self.errorResponse(request)
+        }
+        viewModel.userAgents = [
+            UserAgent(id: "agent1", name: "X", emoji: "🦊", isDefault: false)
+        ]
+
+        let failures = await viewModel.installAgents(["agent1"])
+
+        #expect(failures.isEmpty)
+        #expect(calls == 0)
+    }
+
+    @Test func installAgentsBailsMidBatchOnCancellation() async throws {
+        // SetupLoadingView wraps installAgents in a Task and cancels on
+        // .onDisappear. The contract that matters is mid-batch
+        // cancellation — the user navigates away while installs are
+        // partway through. A cancel-before-start test only proves the
+        // trivial case; this test deterministically waits for the first
+        // install request to actually start, THEN cancels.
+        //
+        // The loop is set up to never complete on its own (operation
+        // returns "running" forever, polling cap effectively infinite),
+        // so the only thing that can make installAgents return is
+        // cancellation observed inside the loop.
+        let createPayload = try operationCreateJSON()
+        let runningPayload = try operationStatusJSON(status: "running")
+
+        let (firstStartedStream, firstStartedContinuation) = AsyncStream<Void>.makeStream()
+        nonisolated(unsafe) var firstSignaled = false
+
+        let viewModel = makeViewModel(
+            handler: { request in
+                let path = request.url?.path ?? ""
+                if path.contains("/install") {
+                    if !firstSignaled {
+                        firstSignaled = true
+                        firstStartedContinuation.yield(())
+                        firstStartedContinuation.finish()
+                    }
+                    return Self.okResponse(request, createPayload)
+                }
+                return Self.okResponse(request, runningPayload)
+            },
+            maxPollAttempts: 1_000_000
+        )
+
+        let task = Task {
+            await viewModel.installAgents(["agent1", "agent2", "agent3"])
+        }
+
+        var iterator = firstStartedStream.makeAsyncIterator()
+        _ = await iterator.next()  // wait until first install actually started
+
+        task.cancel()
+        let failures = await task.value  // returns because cancellation observed
+
+        // Cancellation isn't surfaced as a failure — the loop just stops.
+        // If installAgents had ignored cancellation, this await would never
+        // return (operation polling is infinite) and the test would hang.
+        #expect(failures.isEmpty)
+    }
+
+    @Test func installAgentsAllFailReturnsAllIds() async throws {
+        let createPayload = try operationCreateJSON()
+        let failedPayload = try operationStatusJSON(status: "failed", errorMessage: "Boom")
+
+        let viewModel = makeViewModel { request in
+            let path = request.url?.path ?? ""
+            if path.contains("/install") {
+                return Self.okResponse(request, createPayload)
+            }
+            return Self.okResponse(request, failedPayload)
+        }
+
+        let failures = await viewModel.installAgents(["agent1", "agent2"])
+
+        // Order isn't guaranteed (Set iteration), so compare as Set.
+        #expect(Set(failures) == Set(["agent1", "agent2"]))
+    }
+
     @Test func successClearsStateAndRefreshesUserAgents() async throws {

         let runningPayload = try operationStatusJSON(status: "running")

+import Testing
+@testable import ZooClaw
+
+/// Pins the entry-action decision logic that drives SetupLoadingView.startSetup.
+/// The .alreadyReady case is the one that previously hung the view: provisioning
+/// completed before SetupLoadingView appeared, so no onChange edge fired and
+/// runInstalls was never invoked.
+struct SetupEntryActionTests {
+
+    @Test func noUidWhenUidIsNil() {
+        let action = SetupEntryAction.decide(
+            uid: nil,
+            botState: .idle,
+            hasStartedProvisioning: false
+        )
+        #expect(action == .noUid)
+    }
+
+    @Test func alreadyReadyWhenBotStateIsReady() {
+        // Regression guard: if provisioning completed before view appeared,
+        // we must trigger installs directly because no .idle → .ready
+        // onChange edge will ever fire from this view's perspective.
+        let action = SetupEntryAction.decide(
+            uid: "uid-1",
+            botState: .ready,
+            hasStartedProvisioning: false
+        )
+        #expect(action == .alreadyReady)
+    }
+
+    @Test func alreadyReadyEvenWhenHasStartedProvisioning() {
+        // Even if we marked provisioning as started elsewhere, .ready at
+        // entry should always trigger the install path — re-entry shouldn't
+        // skip it.
+        let action = SetupEntryAction.decide(
+            uid: "uid-1",
+            botState: .ready,
+            hasStartedProvisioning: true
+        )
+        #expect(action == .alreadyReady)
+    }
+
+    @Test func startProvisioningWhenIdleAndNotStarted() {
+        let action = SetupEntryAction.decide(
+            uid: "uid-1",
+            botState: .idle,
+            hasStartedProvisioning: false
+        )
+        #expect(action == .startProvisioning)
+    }
+
+    @Test func alreadyInProgressWhenIdleButAlreadyStarted() {
+        let action = SetupEntryAction.decide(
+            uid: "uid-1",
+            botState: .idle,
+            hasStartedProvisioning: true
+        )
+        #expect(action == .alreadyInProgress)
+    }
+
+    @Test func alreadyInProgressWhenProvisioning() {
+        let action = SetupEntryAction.decide(
+            uid: "uid-1",
+            botState: .provisioning(phase: .initializing),
+            hasStartedProvisioning: true
+        )
+        #expect(action == .alreadyInProgress)
+    }
+
+    @Test func alreadyInProgressWhenError() {
+        // Error state isn't a separate entry case — startSetup leaves the
+        // error UI to render; entry just bumps progress.
+        let action = SetupEntryAction.decide(
+            uid: "uid-1",
+            botState: .error("boom"),
+            hasStartedProvisioning: false
+        )
+        #expect(action == .alreadyInProgress)
+    }
+}

commit.message:
feat(openclaw): support imported agent installs (#1470)

## Summary
- add import agent support to OpenClaw install and uninstall request
handling
- persist imported agents as custom metadata and preserve their source
in backend responses
- extend web API helpers and unit tests for import async operations and
validation

## Context
- enables import-based agent installs to use raw remote archive URLs end
to end

- extend web API helpers and unit tests for import async operations and

commit.message:
chore: remove unused R2 private bucket config and code (#1472)

## Summary
- Remove unused `enterprise-user-data` private bucket config — no code
path ever passes `is_private=True`
- Remove `R2_PRIVATE_BUCKET_NAME`, `R2_PRIVATE_ACCESS_KEY_ID`,
`R2_PRIVATE_ACCESS_KEY_SECRET` from settings, env, and CI
- Simplify `R2StorageClient` from dual-client (public + private) to
single client
- Remove `is_private` / `supportsPrivate` plumbing from frontend
presigned URL factory

## Files changed (10)
- `.env.example` — remove 3 R2_PRIVATE entries
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — remove R2_PRIVATE vars/secrets (5
references)
- `services/claw-interface/app/settings.py` — remove 3 fields
- `services/claw-interface/app/services/r2_storage.py` — simplify to
single s3 client
- `services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_r2_storage.py` — remove
private tests
- `web/src/lib/r2/client.ts` — remove private config and branch
- `web/src/app/[locale]/_presignedFactory.ts` — remove
supportsPrivate/isPrivate
- `web/src/app/[locale]/image/presigned/route.ts` — remove isPrivate
param
- `web/src/app/[locale]/video/presigned/route.ts` — remove
supportsPrivate
- `web/tests/unit/app/image-video-presigned.unit.spec.ts` — remove
private test

## Test plan
- [ ] CI passes (backend + frontend)
- [ ] Existing image/video upload still works (no functional change —
private path was never used)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: defense-in-depth auth & clickjacking headers (exclude mini-chat embed) (#1436)

## Summary
- **admin_cron**: add `require_internal_service_key` dependency to all 6
cron trigger/query endpoints (defense-in-depth behind CF Zero Trust)
- **orders/admin/grant**: add `require_admin_user` dependency
- **next.config**: add `X-Frame-Options: DENY` +
`Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none'` (clickjacking
protection)
- **billing_gateway**: retry `topup_wallet` once on 5xx with 1s backoff
(Lago transient error resilience)

## Context
Production incident 2026-04-27: Lago returned a transient 500 on `POST
/api/v1/wallet_transactions` during a user's subscription grant. The
user saw "Failed to confirm order" and had to re-purchase on a lower
plan. The topup retry prevents this class of failure from reaching the
user.

Security hardening prompted by external vulnerability reports — CF Zero
Trust already protects `/admin/*`, but application-layer auth adds
defense-in-depth. `X-Frame-Options` addresses a reported clickjacking
concern.

## Test plan
- [ ] `pytest tests/unit/test_stripe_billing_gateway.py` — 15 tests pass
(2 new: 500 retry success + 500 retry then raise)
- [ ] `pyright` clean on all changed files
- [ ] Verify cron scheduler passes `X-Internal-Service-Key` header
(otherwise cron triggers will 401)
- [ ] Verify `/admin/grant` still works from admin UI (JWT auth)
- [ ] Verify `zooclaw.ai` pages return `X-Frame-Options: DENY` header
after deploy

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

       }
     : {}),
 
+  // Security headers — clickjacking protection
+  async headers() {
+    return [
+      {
+        // Exclude /mini-chat — it is designed to be embedded in iframes
+        source: '/((?!(?:[^/]+/)?mini-chat(?:/|$)).*)',
+        headers: [
+          { key: 'X-Frame-Options', value: 'DENY' },
+          { key: 'Content-Security-Policy', value: "frame-ancestors 'none'" },
+        ],
+      },
+    ]
+  },
+
   // 压缩优化
   compress: true,

commit.message:
feat(claw-interface): add R2 presigned URL endpoint for frontend (#1465)

## Summary
- Add `GET /api/storage/r2/presign` endpoint for frontend to obtain
presigned upload URLs
- Uses existing `R2StorageClient.generate_presigned_put_url` — no extra
network hop to ecap-proxy-service
- JWT auth via `Depends(get_current_user)` at router level
- Companion to SerendipityOneInc/ecap-proxy-service#39 (same feature on
proxy side)

## API

```
GET /api/storage/r2/presign?filename=photo.png&file_type=image&content_type=image/png
Authorization: Bearer <jwt>

Response:
{
  "presigned_url": "https://r2.../presigned?sig=...",
  "public_url": "https://cdn.example.com/image/20260429/abc.png"
}
```

## Files Changed
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `services/claw-interface/app/routes/storage.py` | New route with
`PresignedUrlResponse` schema |
| `services/claw-interface/app/create_app.py` | Register
`storage.router` |
| `services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_storage.py` | 9 unit tests |

## Test plan
- [x] All pre-commit hooks pass (ruff, pyright, import-linter)
- [ ] CI unit tests pass
- [ ] Manual test via telepresence after deploy

@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+"""Routes for R2 storage presigned URL generation."""
+
+import logging
+
+from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query
+from pydantic import BaseModel
+
+from app.auth.dependencies import get_current_user
+from app.services.r2_storage import get_r2_client
+
+router = APIRouter(prefix="/storage", tags=["storage"], dependencies=[Depends(get_current_user)])
+
+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+
+# Default extension when filename has no dot
+_DEFAULT_EXTENSION = "bin"
+
+
+class PresignedUrlResponse(BaseModel):
+    """Response for presigned URL generation."""
+
+    presigned_url: str
+    public_url: str
+
+
+@router.get("/r2/presign", response_model=PresignedUrlResponse, operation_id="generate_r2_presigned_url")
+async def presign_r2(
+    filename: str = Query(..., description="Original filename. Extension is extracted for object-key generation."),
+    file_type: str = Query(
+        ..., description="File type, used as the R2 object-key prefix. E.g. image, video, audio, document."
+    ),
+) -> PresignedUrlResponse:
+    """Generate a presigned URL for direct client upload to Cloudflare R2.
+
+    The client receives a ``presigned_url`` and uses HTTP **PUT** to upload the
+    file directly to R2, bypassing the backend proxy.  The ``public_url`` is the
+    CDN-accessible address available immediately after upload.
+
+    Auth: ``Authorization: Bearer <user-jwt>``
+    """
+    try:
+        extension = filename.rsplit(".", 1)[-1] if "." in filename else _DEFAULT_EXTENSION
+        r2 = get_r2_client()
+        presigned_url, public_url = r2.generate_presigned_put_url(
+            purpose=file_type,
+            extension=extension,
+        )
+        return PresignedUrlResponse(presigned_url=presigned_url, public_url=public_url)
+    except Exception as e:
+        logger.error("Presign generation failed: %s", e, exc_info=True)
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Presign generation failed") from e

@@ -0,0 +1,81 @@
+"""Unit tests for storage presigned URL route."""
+
+from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
+
+import pytest
+from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
+
+from app.auth.dependencies import get_current_user
+from app.create_app import create_app
+
+from ._builders import make_current_user
+
+
+@pytest.fixture
+def client():
+    app = create_app(include_metrics=False)
+    app.dependency_overrides[get_current_user] = make_current_user
+    return TestClient(app, raise_server_exceptions=False)
+
+
+@pytest.fixture
+def mock_r2():
+    with patch("app.routes.storage.get_r2_client") as mock_get:
+        mock_client = MagicMock()
+        mock_client.generate_presigned_put_url.return_value = (
+            "https://r2.example.com/presigned?sig=abc",
+            "https://cdn.example.com/image/20260429/abc.png",
+        )
+        mock_get.return_value = mock_client
+        yield mock_client
+
+
+class TestPresignR2:
+    """Tests for GET /storage/r2/presign."""
+
+    def test_presign_success_default_purpose(self, client: TestClient, mock_r2: MagicMock):
+        response = client.get("/storage/r2/presign", params={"filename": "photo.png", "file_type": "image"})
+
+        assert response.status_code == 200
+        data = response.json()
+        assert data["presigned_url"] == "https://r2.example.com/presigned?sig=abc"
+        assert data["public_url"] == "https://cdn.example.com/image/20260429/abc.png"
+        mock_r2.generate_presigned_put_url.assert_called_once_with(purpose="image", extension="png")
+
+    def test_presign_extracts_extension(self, client: TestClient, mock_r2: MagicMock):
+        client.get("/storage/r2/presign", params={"filename": "clip.mp4", "file_type": "ecap_video"})
+
+        mock_r2.generate_presigned_put_url.assert_called_once_with(purpose="ecap_video", extension="mp4")
+
+    def test_presign_fallback_extension_when_no_dot(self, client: TestClient, mock_r2: MagicMock):
+        client.get("/storage/r2/presign", params={"filename": "noext", "file_type": "user_upload"})
+
+        mock_r2.generate_presigned_put_url.assert_called_once_with(purpose="user_upload", extension="bin")
+
+    def test_presign_with_custom_purpose(self, client: TestClient, mock_r2: MagicMock):
+        client.get("/storage/r2/presign", params={"filename": "model.glb", "file_type": "ai_generated_image"})
+
+        mock_r2.generate_presigned_put_url.assert_called_once_with(purpose="ai_generated_image", extension="glb")
+
+    def test_presign_missing_params_returns_422(self, client: TestClient):
+        response = client.get("/storage/r2/presign")
+        assert response.status_code == 422
+
+    def test_presign_failure_returns_500(self, client: TestClient):
+        with patch("app.routes.storage.get_r2_client") as mock_get:
+            mock_get.return_value.generate_presigned_put_url.side_effect = Exception("R2 error")
+            response = client.get("/storage/r2/presign", params={"filename": "test.png", "file_type": "image"})
+
+        assert response.status_code == 500
+        assert response.json()["detail"] == "Presign generation failed"
+
+
+class TestPresignAuth:
+    """Auth is applied at router level via dependencies=[Depends(get_current_user)]."""
+
+    def test_presign_401_without_auth(self):
+        no_auth_app = create_app(include_metrics=False)
+        no_auth_client = TestClient(no_auth_app, raise_server_exceptions=False)
+
+        response = no_auth_client.get("/storage/r2/presign", params={"filename": "test.png", "file_type": "image"})
+        assert response.status_code == 401

chore: remove unused LITELLM_AUTH_TOKEN from .env.example (#1466)

## Summary
- Remove `LITELLM_AUTH_TOKEN` from `.env.example` — it is not consumed
by any code (`settings.py` has no such field, no business logic
references it)
- The corresponding LiteLLM key (`sk-MRwyZJ8ZT7pAIOoESdRwsg`) has been
confirmed invalid (401 `token_not_found_in_db`)
- K8s deployments (`claw-interface`, `ecommerce-studio-service`) still
inject this env var but it is dead config — will be cleaned up
separately in infra

commit.message:
fix(auth): require srp.one user for internal service key (#1459)

## Summary
- require internal service key requests to include a valid Bearer user
token
- allow internal mutation endpoints only when the authenticated user
email ends with `@srp.one`
- reject the legacy flow that passed only the service key in the
`Authorization` header

## Context
- internal mutation access should be bound to an authenticated internal
account, not only a shared service key

files.patch:
@@ -91,7 +91,7 @@ async def require_internal_service_key(
     x_internal_service_key: str | None = Header(default=None),
     authorization: str | None = Header(default=None),
 ) -> str:
-    """Authenticate internal mutation calls using service keys only."""
+    """Authenticate internal mutation calls using a service key plus @srp.one JWT."""
     keys = {k.strip() for k in (SETTINGS.INTERNAL_SERVICE_KEYS or "").split(",") if k.strip()}
     if not keys:
         raise HTTPException(status_code=503, detail="Internal service key auth is not configured")
@@ -105,4 +105,25 @@ async def require_internal_service_key(
     if candidate not in keys:
         raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Invalid internal service key")
 
+    if not authorization:
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Authentication required")
+
+    parts = authorization.split(" ", 1)
+    if len(parts) != 2 or parts[0].lower() != "bearer":  # noqa: PLR2004 — structural: split(" ", 1) yields [scheme, token]
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Invalid authorization header format")
+
+    token = parts[1].strip()
+    if not token:
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Authentication required")
+
+    # If callers still pass the service key via Authorization, they cannot also
+    # provide the required user JWT for the @srp.one gate.
+    if token == candidate and not x_internal_service_key:
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Bearer user token required when using internal service key")
+
+    user_info = await verify_user_token(token)
+    email = str(user_info.get("email") or "").strip().lower()
+    if not email.endswith("@srp.one"):
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Internal service key requires an @srp.one account")
+
     return candidate

@@ -54,6 +54,73 @@ async def test_get_current_uid_returns_uid(self):
         assert result == "uid-42"
 
 
+class TestRequireInternalServiceKey:
+    """Tests for app.auth.dependencies.require_internal_service_key."""
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_valid_service_key_requires_srpone_email(self):
+        from app.auth.dependencies import require_internal_service_key
+
+        with (
+            patch("app.auth.dependencies.SETTINGS.INTERNAL_SERVICE_KEYS", "svc-key-1,svc-key-2"),
+            patch(
+                "app.auth.dependencies.verify_user_token",
+                AsyncMock(return_value={"uid": "u1", "email": "dev@srp.one"}),
+            ),
+        ):
+            result = await require_internal_service_key(
+                x_internal_service_key="svc-key-2",
+                authorization="Bearer user-jwt",
+            )
+
+        assert result == "svc-key-2"
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_non_srpone_email_rejected(self):
+        from app.auth.dependencies import require_internal_service_key
+
+        with (
+            patch("app.auth.dependencies.SETTINGS.INTERNAL_SERVICE_KEYS", "svc-key"),
+            patch(
+                "app.auth.dependencies.verify_user_token",
+                AsyncMock(return_value={"uid": "u1", "email": "dev@example.com"}),
+            ),
+        ):
+            with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc:
+                await require_internal_service_key(
+                    x_internal_service_key="svc-key",
+                    authorization="Bearer user-jwt",
+                )
+
+        assert exc.value.status_code == 403
+        assert "@srp.one" in exc.value.detail
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_missing_authorization_rejected_even_with_valid_service_key(self):
+        from app.auth.dependencies import require_internal_service_key
+
+        with patch("app.auth.dependencies.SETTINGS.INTERNAL_SERVICE_KEYS", "svc-key"):
+            with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc:
+                await require_internal_service_key(x_internal_service_key="svc-key", authorization=None)
+
+        assert exc.value.status_code == 401
+        assert "Authentication required" in exc.value.detail
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_authorization_cannot_be_service_key_without_user_jwt(self):
+        from app.auth.dependencies import require_internal_service_key
+
+        with patch("app.auth.dependencies.SETTINGS.INTERNAL_SERVICE_KEYS", "svc-key"):
+            with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc:
+                await require_internal_service_key(
+                    x_internal_service_key=None,
+                    authorization="Bearer svc-key",
+                )
+
+        assert exc.value.status_code == 401
+        assert "Bearer user token required" in exc.value.detail
+
+
 class TestVerifyUserToken:

commit.message:
feat(ios): Pre-auth onboarding redesign with role + use-case screens (#1458)

## Summary

**PR-A of 2** in the onboarding redesign split. Builds on the
infrastructure landed in #1452 and ships the redesigned **pre-auth**
flow: hero → name → role → use case → register.

This PR was extracted from #1450 to fit the 2000-line PR size budget.
PR-B (forthcoming) will ship the post-auth view redesigns:
EmailOTPLoginView, AgentSelectView, SetupLoadingView, and the full
OnboardingFlowView refactor.

### Pre-auth view redesigns

- **HeroView** — video background loop, Montserrat + Instrument Serif
tagline.
- **NameInputView** — editorial italic-serif underline input, panda
avatar, Continue pinned bottom.
- **RoleSelectionView** *(new)* — six-card grid; default-first with VM
rehydration on back-nav.
- **UseCaseSelectionView** *(new)* — four-card grid; same rehydration
pattern.
- **RegisterView** — simplified email-only entry with Google / Apple
SSO, inline terms/privacy.

### VM scaffolding

- New `roleSelection` / `useCaseSelection` enum cases.
- `selectedRole` / `selectedUseCase` storage (drives the rehydration on
back-nav).
- `selectedAgentIds: Set<String>` with `maxSelectedAgents = 3`.
- `goBack` table aligned with `canGoBack` (no dead branches).

### NotificationsView fix

PR #1452 deleted three onboarding notification imagesets but main's
NotificationsView still references them. This PR ships the redesigned
view (logo_black + animated cards) to restore main's visual integrity
rather than leaving it broken until PR-B.

### Compatibility shims

`AgentSelectView` and `SetupLoadingView` keep their main-version designs
but are minimally updated to consume `selectedAgentIds: Set<String>`
(replacing the dropped `selectedAgentId: String?`). Their full redesigns
ship in PR-B.

## Tests

- 28/28 OnboardingViewModelTests pass.
- New coverage: rehydration, set mutations, `maxSelectedAgents`, new
screen navigation.

## Test plan

- [ ] Onboarding pre-auth flow end-to-end: hero → name → role → use case
→ register
- [ ] Back-navigation from role/use-case restores prior selection
(rehydration)
- [ ] Notifications screen renders correctly (logo + animated cards, no
missing images)
- [ ] Agent select still works end-to-end (compat shim consuming
`selectedAgentIds.first`)
- [ ] Existing chat / agents / settings flows unchanged

## Notes

- Size: **1133 / 2000 lines** — comfortable budget, no `size-override`
needed.
- Mid-flow visual mismatch is expected on TestFlight builds between this
PR and PR-B (new pre-auth + old EmailOTP / AgentSelect / SetupLoading).
- Supersedes the corresponding portions of #1450; #1450 will be closed
or rebased once both halves land.

@@ -5,6 +5,8 @@ class OnboardingViewModel {
     enum Screen: Equatable {
         case hero
         case nameInput
+        case roleSelection
+        case useCaseSelection

     var screen: Screen = .hero
     var userName: String = ""
-    var selectedAgentId: String?
+    var selectedRole: String?
+    var selectedUseCase: String?
+    var selectedAgentIds: Set<String> = []
+    static let maxSelectedAgents = 3
     /// Only true when createUser was called (brand new user going through full onboarding)
     var isNewUser: Bool = false
 
+    /// Set true once the user advances *or* skips a step. Lets the view's
+    /// init distinguish "never visited → seed default" from "explicitly
+    /// skipped → keep nil"; without this a skip reverts to the first option
+    /// on rehydration.
+    var roleVisited: Bool = false
+    var useCaseVisited: Bool = false
+
     func goToNameInput() {
         screen = .nameInput
     }
 
+    func goToRoleSelection() {
+        screen = .roleSelection
+    }
+
+    func goToUseCaseSelection() {
+        screen = .useCaseSelection
+    }
+
     func goToAgentSelect() {
         screen = .agentSelect
     }
@@ -40,7 +60,7 @@ class OnboardingViewModel {
 
     var canGoBack: Bool {
         switch screen {
-        case .nameInput, .emailOTP, .agentSelect:
+        case .nameInput, .roleSelection, .useCaseSelection, .emailOTP:
             return true
         default:
             return false
@@ -51,17 +71,33 @@ class OnboardingViewModel {
         switch screen {
         case .nameInput:
             screen = .hero
-        case .emailOTP:
-            screen = .register
-        case .register:
+        case .roleSelection:
             screen = .nameInput
-        case .agentSelect:
+        case .useCaseSelection:
+            screen = .roleSelection
+        case .emailOTP:
             screen = .register
-        case .notifications:
-            screen = .agentSelect
         default:
+            // agentSelect / notifications / setupLoading are post-auth — back
+            // would cross the auth boundary and force the user through register
+            // and OTP again, so we don't navigate.
             break
         }
     }
 
+    /// Reset every onboarding-scoped piece of state. Call on sign-out so a
+    /// later sign-in cycle doesn't rehydrate the previous session's role /
+    /// use-case / name answers — the VM is `@State` on the app shell and
+    /// persists across sign-out/in, so without this, stale values leak
+    /// into the new flow.
+    func reset() {
+        screen = .hero
+        userName = ""
+        selectedRole = nil
+        selectedUseCase = nil
+        selectedAgentIds = []
+        isNewUser = false
+        roleVisited = false
+        useCaseVisited = false
+    }
 }

+import SwiftUI
+
+struct RoleSelectionView: View {
+    @Bindable var viewModel: OnboardingViewModel
+
+    @State private var selectedRole: Role?
+
+    private typealias Onb = AppTheme.Onboarding
+
+    init(viewModel: OnboardingViewModel) {
+        self.viewModel = viewModel
+        // Seed the default-first only on the user's FIRST visit. After they've
+        // advanced or skipped once, honor the stored value — including nil
+        // (skipped) — so back-navigation doesn't silently overwrite a skip
+        // with the first role.
+        let initial: Role?
+        if viewModel.roleVisited {
+            initial = viewModel.selectedRole.flatMap(Role.init(rawValue:))
+        } else {
+            initial = Role.allCases.first
+        }
+        self._selectedRole = State(initialValue: initial)
+    }
+
+    private let columns = [
+        GridItem(.flexible(), spacing: 16),
+        GridItem(.flexible(), spacing: 16)
+    ]
+
+    var body: some View {
+        ZStack {
+            AppTheme.onboardingImageBackgroundView()
+                .ignoresSafeArea()
+
+            VStack(spacing: 0) {
+                Spacer().frame(height: 24)
+
+                // Title
+                Text("What best describes you?")
+                    .font(.custom("HankenGrotesk-Bold", size: 34))
+                    .foregroundStyle(Onb.textPrimary)
+                    .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .leading)
+                    .padding(.horizontal, 24)
+
+                Spacer().frame(height: 20)
+
+                // Role cards grid
+                LazyVGrid(columns: columns, spacing: 16) {
+                    ForEach(Role.allCases) { role in
+                        RoleCardView(
+                            role: role,
+                            isSelected: selectedRole == role
+                        )
+                        .onTapGesture { selectedRole = role }
+                    }
+                }
+                .padding(.horizontal, 24)
+
+                Spacer()
+
+                // Continue + Skip
+                VStack(spacing: 16) {
+                    PrimaryButton("Continue", disabled: selectedRole == nil) {
+                        advance()
+                    }
+
+                    Button {
+                        skip()
+                    } label: {
+                        Text("Skip for now")
+                            .font(.custom("Inter-Medium", size: 15))
+                            .foregroundStyle(Onb.textSecondary)
+                    }
+                    .padding(.bottom, 12)
+                }
+            }
+        }
+    }
+
+    // MARK: - Actions
+
+    private func advance() {
+        guard let selectedRole else { return }
+        viewModel.selectedRole = selectedRole.rawValue
+        viewModel.roleVisited = true
+        viewModel.goToUseCaseSelection()
+    }
+
+    private func skip() {
+        viewModel.selectedRole = nil
+        viewModel.roleVisited = true
+        viewModel.goToUseCaseSelection()
+    }
+}
+
+// MARK: - Role
+
+enum Role: String, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
+    case smallBusinessOwner = "Small Business Owner"
+    case kolCreator = "KOL / Creator"
+    case officeWorker = "Office Worker"
+    case entrepreneur = "Entrepreneur"
+    case freelancer = "Freelancer"
+    case others = "Others"
+
+    var id: String { rawValue }
+
+    var imageName: String {
+        switch self {
+        case .officeWorker: "job_office_worker"
+        case .smallBusinessOwner: "job_small_business_owner"
+        case .freelancer: "job_freelancer"
+        case .entrepreneur: "job_entrepreneur"
+        case .kolCreator: "job_kol"
+        case .others: "job_others"
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+// MARK: - Role Card
+
+private struct RoleCardView: View {
+    private typealias Onb = AppTheme.Onboarding
+    let role: Role
+    let isSelected: Bool
+
+    var body: some View {
+        VStack(spacing: 10) {
+            Image(role.imageName)
+                .resizable()
+                .scaledToFit()
+                .frame(width: 72, height: 72)
+
+            Text(role.rawValue)
+                .font(.custom("Inter-SemiBold", size: 14))
+                .fontWeight(.semibold)
+                .foregroundStyle(AppTheme.textPrimary)
+                .multilineTextAlignment(.center)
+                .lineLimit(2)
+                .minimumScaleFactor(0.8)
+        }
+        .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
+        .frame(height: 134)
+        .background(
+            RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 14)
+                .fill(isSelected ? AppTheme.bgPage : .white)
+        )
+        .overlay(
+            RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 14)
+                .strokeBorder(
+                    isSelected ? Onb.textPrimary : Onb.selectionBorder,
+                    lineWidth: isSelected ? 1.5 : 1
+                )
+        )
+        .overlay(alignment: .topTrailing) {
+            if isSelected {
+                Image(systemName: "checkmark.circle.fill")
+                    .font(.system(size: 20))
+                    .foregroundStyle(Onb.textPrimary)
+                    .padding(8)
+            }
+        }
+        .contentShape(Rectangle())
+        .animation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.15), value: isSelected)
+    }
+}

commit.message:
fix(web): AgentDetailClient Escape key listener race (#1275) (#1461)

## Summary

`AgentDetailClient` 的 Escape 处理是 `useCallback(handler, [showConfirm,
showSuccess, ...])` + `useEffect(addEventListener, [handleKeyDown])` ——
每次 modal state 翻转都会回收旧 listener、附新 listener。问题是 `confirm → await
hireAgent → setShowSuccess(true)` 这条 async 路径里,success modal 进
DOM(commit)和 listener 重新挂载是两步。中间窗口里 Escape 落到旧 closure(`showSuccess:
false`),什么都不关。

改成 ref pattern:listener 挂载一次(empty deps),每次 render 把最新 state 写到
ref;handler 调用时读 ref。优先级 / 触发链都不变。

## Repro / 验证

- 之前:`Escape key closes modals (priority order) > success open → Escape
closes success` 在 16 轮 full-suite 跑里偶发 fail ~12%
- 修复后本地 16 轮单文件全过(`pnpm test:unit AgentDetailClient.unit.spec.tsx`)

## 关闭

Closes #1275

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test:unit
tests/unit/app/agents-manager/AgentDetailClient.unit.spec.tsx` × 16
轮全过(31 tests)
- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` 全仓 clean
- [x] `npx eslint
src/app/[locale]/agents-manager/[id]/AgentDetailClient.tsx` clean
- [x] `npx prettier --check` clean
- [ ] CI: code-quality / lint-and-test

false`),什么都不关。

改成 ref pattern:listener 挂载一次(empty deps),每次 render 把最新 state 写到
ref;handler 调用时读 ref。优先级 / 触发链都不变。

## Repro / 验证

- 之前:`Escape key closes modals (priority order) > success open → Escape
closes success` 在 16 轮 full-suite 跑里偶发 fail ~12%
- 修复后本地 16 轮单文件全过(`pnpm test:unit AgentDetailClient.unit.spec.tsx`)

## 关闭

Closes #1275

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test:unit
tests/unit/app/agents-manager/AgentDetailClient.unit.spec.tsx` × 16
轮全过(31 tests)
- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` 全仓 clean
- [x] `npx eslint
src/app/[locale]/agents-manager/[id]/AgentDetailClient.tsx` clean
- [x] `npx prettier --check` clean
- [ ] CI: code-quality / lint-and-test

@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 'use client'
 
 import { BoltIcon, ClockIcon, LinkIcon } from '@heroicons/react/24/outline'
-import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
+import { useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'
 
 import ClawPageHeader from '@/components/ClawPageHeader'
 import LocaleLink from '@/components/LocaleLink'
@@ -148,24 +148,36 @@ export default function AgentDetailClient({ agentId }: { agentId: string }) {
     }
   }
 
-  // Close modals on Escape
-  const handleKeyDown = useCallback(
-    (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
-      if (e.key === 'Escape') {
-        if (showSuccess) setShowSuccess(false)
-        else if (showConfirm) setShowConfirm(false)
-        else if (showFireConfirm) setShowFireConfirm(false)
-        else if (showFired) setShowFired(false)
-        else if (showUpdateConfirm) setShowUpdateConfirm(false)
-        else if (showUpdated && !isResetting) setShowUpdated(false)
-      }
-    },
-    [showConfirm, showSuccess, showFireConfirm, showFired, showUpdateConfirm, showUpdated, isResetting],
-  )
+  // Close modals on Escape (priority: success > confirm > fireConfirm > fired > updateConfirm > updated).
+  // Bind once via ref instead of re-registering per state change — the cleanup→re-attach
+  // window races with React commits when state updates come from async callbacks
+  // (confirm → await hireAgent → setShowSuccess), so a fast Escape would otherwise hit a
+  // stale closure where the freshly-shown modal looks closed (#1275).
+  const modalState = {
+    showConfirm,
+    showSuccess,
+    showFireConfirm,
+    showFired,
+    showUpdateConfirm,
+    showUpdated,
+    isResetting,
+  }
+  const modalStateRef = useRef(modalState)
+  modalStateRef.current = modalState
   useEffect(() => {
-    document.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown)
-    return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown)
-  }, [handleKeyDown])
+    const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
+      if (e.key !== 'Escape') return
+      const s = modalStateRef.current
+      if (s.showSuccess) setShowSuccess(false)
+      else if (s.showConfirm) setShowConfirm(false)
+      else if (s.showFireConfirm) setShowFireConfirm(false)
+      else if (s.showFired) setShowFired(false)
+      else if (s.showUpdateConfirm) setShowUpdateConfirm(false)
+      else if (s.showUpdated && !s.isResetting) setShowUpdated(false)
+    }
+    document.addEventListener('keydown', handler)
+    return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handler)
+  }, [])
 
   const r = (tmpl: string, name: string) => tmpl.replace('{name}', name)

@@ -484,6 +484,30 @@ describe('AgentDetailClient', () => {
       fireEvent.keyDown(document, { key: 'Escape' })
       await waitFor(() => expect(view.queryByTestId('hire-success-modal')).not.toBeInTheDocument())
     })
+
+    // Structural regression for #1275: the previous useCallback+useEffect pattern
+    // re-attached the listener on every modal-state flip, racing with React commits
+    // when state came from async callbacks (confirm → await hireAgent → setShowSuccess).
+    // Counting the registrations is deterministic; the timing flake itself isn't.
+    it('keydown listener binds once across modal state transitions (#1275)', async () => {
+      const addSpy = vi.spyOn(document, 'addEventListener')
+      const removeSpy = vi.spyOn(document, 'removeEventListener')
+      try {
+        hoisted.hireAgent.mockResolvedValue(undefined)
+        const view = await renderReady()
+        fireEvent.click(view.getByText(/zooSquare.hire/))
+        fireEvent.click(view.getByText('zooSquare.confirm'))
+        await waitFor(() => view.getByTestId('hire-success-modal'))
+        const keydownAdds = addSpy.mock.calls.filter(([type]) => type === 'keydown').length
+        expect(keydownAdds).toBe(1)
+        view.unmount()
+        const keydownRemoves = removeSpy.mock.calls.filter(([type]) => type === 'keydown').length
+        expect(keydownRemoves).toBe(1)
+      } finally {
+        addSpy.mockRestore()
+        removeSpy.mockRestore()
+      }
+    })
   })
 
   // ── Syncing modal ──

commit.message:
chore(web): migrate 11 files from inline style to Tailwind (#754) (#1457)

## Summary

- 迁 11 个 grandfathered 文件 / 21 处 inline `style` → Tailwind utility
- `eslint.config.mjs` `react/forbid-dom-props` ignores: **45 →
34**(shrink-only sentinel 通过)
- 推进 #754 长 tracker;选品依据:**当前 ignores 中非 branded、纯静态可 TW 化的候选**

## Files

**Batch 1 — 5 files / 12 处**
- `PublicFooter.tsx` (5): h-8/w-auto, mt-2, h-5/w-5 ×2, justify-start
- `PublicHeader.tsx` (2): inline-flex/items-center/gap-1.5,
block/text-center
- `PlanCard.tsx` (3): w-[0.6em]/h-[1.15em]/align-bottom, leading-none
×2, layered absolute layout
- `InputArea.tsx` (1): hidden
- `FeedbackDialog.tsx` (1): max-h-[min(500px,70vh)]

**Batch 2 — 6 files / 9 处**
- `ThinkingIndicator.tsx` (3): [animation-delay:0s/0.2s/0.4s]
- `ArchivedSessionPanel.tsx` (1): min-h-[500px]
- `AssetsPanel.tsx` (1): aspect-[3/4]
- `UserMenu.tsx` (1): border-border(去掉冗余 var 引用)
- `DowngradeConfirmModal.tsx` (1):
bg-[var(--ecap-billing-badge-current)]
- `MarkdownContent.tsx` (1): [overflow-wrap:anywhere],并删去冗余的
wordBreak(已被 className `break-words` 覆盖)

## Notes

- Issue 原列的 top-5(LoginForm / verify / GeneralTab / PaywallContent /
ChannelStep)早已被前序 opportunistic PR 清理完毕,已不在 ignores 列表里。
- 当前 ignores 中量最大的几个文件(`CompanionSelectStep` 11 处、`InviteCodeStep` 9
处、`OnboardingLayout` 7 处等)都是 **branded 模块** 内的 `rgba(...)` / `#hex` 颜色,被
#796 theme refactor 阻塞,本 PR 跳过。
- **Audit gotcha**:ESLint `react/forbid-dom-props` 同时拦截 `style={var}`(单
brace)和 `style={{...}}`(双 brace)。第一轮 grep `'style={{'` 漏掉了
`MMAttachments.tsx` 里 3 处 `style={boxStyle}` 的 LEGIT 数据驱动样式(image 尺寸
from runtime metadata)。MMAttachments 保留在 ignores 内,留待后续 PR 加
inline-disable + 原因注释。
- 所有迁移都是 1:1 映射(rem 基于 root 16px);`break-words` Tailwind 即 `word-break:
break-word`,所以原 inline 的 `wordBreak: 'break-word'` 是冗余,删去。

## Test plan

- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` clean
- [x] `npx eslint <files>` clean
- [x] `bash scripts/check-ignores-shrink-only.sh` pass(45 → 34)
- [x] `npx vitest run` 12 spec / 279 tests pass
- [ ] Visual sanity:landing footer/header,billing PlanCard 价格滚动 +
DowngradeConfirmModal,FeedbackDialog 弹窗,UserMenu
虚线分隔,ArchivedSessionPanel 列表高度,AssetsPanel 卡片纵横比,MarkdownContent 长 URL
换行,ThinkingIndicator 三点交错动画

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(web): RQ v2 PR-a — useUserAgents 切 useQuery (facade 不变) (#1349)

## 背景

按
[`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-react-query-migration-v2.md`](../blob/main/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-react-query-migration-v2.md)
落地 **PR-a**(spec 拆分中的第一个,无依赖,后续 PR-b/c/d/e/f 均建在其上)。

v2 spec 要清扫的是 v1 没覆盖的"包了 `lib/api/*` wrapper 但仍 `useEffect + setState`"的
~34 个文件——它们已经避开了 v1 的 raw `fetch()` lint guard,但 RQ 的 dedup / cache /
focus refetch / 自动重试一项没拿到。本 PR 是这条尾巴的第一刀。

## 改动范围

| 文件 | 操作 | 说明 |
|---|---|---|
| `src/hooks/queries/openclaw/keys.ts` | 新建 | openclaw domain query-key
工厂,uid 进 key(D4: 跨账号缓存隔离) |
| `src/hooks/useUserAgents.ts` | 重写 hook 内部 | `useState + 3×useEffect` →
`useQuery`;模块级 `refreshUserAgentsCache` 整体保留 |
| `tests/unit/hooks/useUserAgents.unit.spec.ts` | 加 createQueryWrapper +
getUserInfo mock | 既有 19 个 case 全过 |

**Public API 完全不变**:`{ agents, isLoading, refreshAgents }` + 模块函数
`refreshUserAgentsCache({ force? })`。9 个 `useUserAgents` callsite + 5 个
`refreshUserAgentsCache` callsite 不动一行。

## 关键决策

### D2 facade 保留 — 内部切到 useQuery
照 v1 PR-7 (`useLiteLLMApi`) 的做法,从消费方看 hook 形状不变,只是内部 fetch 路径换成 RQ。这样 PR
范围 = 2 个文件改动,不是 14 个 callsite 联动。

### 模块级 `refreshUserAgentsCache` 暂不迁
该函数的 `_inflight` / `_lastResult` / `THROTTLE_MS` 对 5 个 imperative
caller(post-mutation 强刷)是契约,把它切成 `queryClient.fetchQuery` 需要先建
`getQueryClient()` 单例,工作量超出本 PR。短期"双轨"通过 `setQueryData(localStorage 值)`
listener 保持一致,后续 PR 再清理。

### `isLoading = query.isPending`(不用 `isFetching`)
旧 hook 的 `isLoading` 只在"无缓存且首次拉"时为 true,跟
`isPending`(还没拿到任何数据)语义匹配。`isFetching` 会在 background refetch / focus
refetch 时也 true,会让 SideNav 等 caller 闪 spinner。

### `initialData + initialDataUpdatedAt: 0`
从 localStorage 缓存读 `initialData` 保留"刷新页面有缓存先显示"的 UX;`updatedAt: 0` 让 RQ
仍把数据视为 stale,mount 时还是会触发网络刷新——两者结合 = 旧版"先缓存,再后台刷新"行为不变。

### Listener 用 `setQueryData` 而非 `invalidateQueries`
`refreshUserAgentsCache` 派发 `'ecap:agents:updated'` 之前已经写好了
localStorage,网络已经 settled。这种情况下 invalidate→refetch 是浪费,直接
`setQueryData(localStorage 值)` 把 RQ cache 跟模块缓存对齐最便宜。跨 tab 的 `storage`
事件同源同处理。

## 验证

- `pnpm tsc --noEmit`:无错
- `pnpm lint`:无报
- `pnpm test:unit tests/unit/hooks/useUserAgents.unit.spec.ts`:19/19
passed
- `pnpm test:unit`(全仓):4183/4183 passed,0 回归

## 不在范围内

- 模块级 `refreshUserAgentsCache` 切 RQ(后续 cleanup PR)
- PR-b: `useClawSettings` / `useBillingCredits` /
`useOfficialAgentCatalog` / `useCustomAgentPublishes`
- PR-c: `claw-settings/` 10 个组件
- PR-d: `chat/` 资源面板 / onboarding / skills 浏览
- PR-e: billing 表面(高风险,串行)
- PR-f: 长尾散点 + 复跑调研命令

## Test plan

- [ ] CI `code-quality / lint-and-test` 绿
- [ ] 手测 `/chat`:agent 列表加载,切 agent 正常
- [ ] SideNav agent 列表显示
- [ ] Skill 详情页 → "添加到我的 agents" → 列表自动刷新(验证 `'ecap:agents:updated'`
事件路径)
- [ ] 退出登录 → 重登(验证 uid 切换 key 重拉)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

从 localStorage 缓存读 `initialData` 保留"刷新页面有缓存先显示"的 UX;`updatedAt: 0` 让 RQ
仍把数据视为 stale,mount 时还是会触发网络刷新——两者结合 = 旧版"先缓存,再后台刷新"行为不变。

### Listener 用 `setQueryData` 而非 `invalidateQueries`
`refreshUserAgentsCache` 派发 `'ecap:agents:updated'` 之前已经写好了
localStorage,网络已经 settled。这种情况下 invalidate→refetch 是浪费,直接
`setQueryData(localStorage 值)` 把 RQ cache 跟模块缓存对齐最便宜。跨 tab 的 `storage`
事件同源同处理。
