+"""Unit tests for admin_events route — uid → team_id resolve, then passthrough to billing_events."""
+
+from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch
+
+import pytest
+from fastapi import HTTPException
+
+from app.routes.admin_events import list_customer_events
+
+
+@pytest.mark.unit
+async def test_resolves_team_id_and_forwards_all_params():
+    """Route looks up uid → team_id, then hands every query param through to the service."""
+    payload = {"events": [{"lago_id": "x"}], "meta": {"total_count": 1}}
+
+    with (
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events.SETTINGS") as mock_settings,
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events.user_repo") as mock_repo,
+        patch(
+            "app.routes.admin_events._list_customer_events",
+            new_callable=AsyncMock,
+            return_value=payload,
+        ) as mock_service,
+    ):
+        mock_settings.BILLING_GATEWAY_URL = "http://bg.test"
+        mock_repo.get_user = AsyncMock(return_value={"uid": "user-1", "team_id": "team-abc"})
+
+        result = await list_customer_events(
+            uid="user-1",
+            timestamp_from="2026-04-21T00:00:00Z",
+            timestamp_to="2026-04-28T23:59:59Z",
+            page=2,
+            per_page=50,
+            _admin_uid="admin-1",
+        )
+
+    assert result == payload
+    # bg keys by team_id, NOT uid — see LAGO_API_CHARGE_BY=team_id convention.
+    mock_service.assert_awaited_once_with(
+        customer_id="team-abc",
+        timestamp_from="2026-04-21T00:00:00Z",
+        timestamp_to="2026-04-28T23:59:59Z",
+        page=2,
+        per_page=50,
+    )
+
+
+@pytest.mark.unit
+async def test_default_params_pass_through_as_none():
+    """No timestamps → forwarded as None so bg applies its 24h default."""
+    with (
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events.SETTINGS") as mock_settings,
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events.user_repo") as mock_repo,
+        patch(
+            "app.routes.admin_events._list_customer_events",
+            new_callable=AsyncMock,
+            return_value={"events": [], "meta": {}},
+        ) as mock_service,
+    ):
+        mock_settings.BILLING_GATEWAY_URL = "http://bg.test"
+        mock_repo.get_user = AsyncMock(return_value={"uid": "user-1", "team_id": "team-abc"})
+
+        # FastAPI materializes Query(...) defaults at request-binding time;
+        # when the handler is called directly we have to pass them explicitly.
+        await list_customer_events(
+            uid="user-1",
+            timestamp_from=None,
+            timestamp_to=None,
+            page=1,
+            per_page=20,
+            _admin_uid="admin-1",
+        )
+
+    mock_service.assert_awaited_once_with(
+        customer_id="team-abc",
+        timestamp_from=None,
+        timestamp_to=None,
+        page=1,
+        per_page=20,
+    )
+
+
+@pytest.mark.unit
+async def test_503_when_bg_not_configured():
+    """Empty BILLING_GATEWAY_URL → 503; user lookup never happens."""
+    with (
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events.SETTINGS") as mock_settings,
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events.user_repo") as mock_repo,
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events._list_customer_events", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_service,
+    ):
+        mock_settings.BILLING_GATEWAY_URL = ""
+        mock_repo.get_user = AsyncMock()
+
+        with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc_info:
+            await list_customer_events(
+                uid="user-1",
+                timestamp_from=None,
+                timestamp_to=None,
+                page=1,
+                per_page=20,
+                _admin_uid="admin-1",
+            )
+
+    assert exc_info.value.status_code == 503
+    mock_repo.get_user.assert_not_called()
+    mock_service.assert_not_called()
+
+
+@pytest.mark.unit
+async def test_404_when_user_not_found():
+    """Unknown uid → 404; bg call never happens."""
+    with (
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events.SETTINGS") as mock_settings,
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events.user_repo") as mock_repo,
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events._list_customer_events", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_service,
+    ):
+        mock_settings.BILLING_GATEWAY_URL = "http://bg.test"
+        mock_repo.get_user = AsyncMock(return_value=None)
+
+        with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc_info:
+            await list_customer_events(
+                uid="ghost",
+                timestamp_from=None,
+                timestamp_to=None,
+                page=1,
+                per_page=20,
+                _admin_uid="admin-1",
+            )
+
+    assert exc_info.value.status_code == 404
+    mock_service.assert_not_called()
+
+
+@pytest.mark.unit
+async def test_returns_empty_envelope_when_user_has_no_team_id():
+    """User exists but isn't bootstrapped to bg → return empty events instead of erroring."""
+    with (
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events.SETTINGS") as mock_settings,
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events.user_repo") as mock_repo,
+        patch("app.routes.admin_events._list_customer_events", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_service,
+    ):
+        mock_settings.BILLING_GATEWAY_URL = "http://bg.test"
+        mock_repo.get_user = AsyncMock(return_value={"uid": "user-1", "team_id": None})
+
+        result = await list_customer_events(
+            uid="user-1",
+            timestamp_from=None,
+            timestamp_to=None,
+            page=1,
+            per_page=20,
+            _admin_uid="admin-1",
+        )
+
+    assert result["events"] == []
+    assert result["meta"]["total_count"] == 0
+    mock_service.assert_not_called()

+"""Unit tests for app.services.billing_events.list_customer_events."""
+
+from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch
+
+import httpx
+import pytest
+
+from app.services.billing_client import BillingGatewayClient
+from app.services.billing_events import list_customer_events
+from tests.unit._http_helpers import make_httpx_response as _mock_response
+
+
+def _mock_http_client(status_code: int = 200, json_data: dict | None = None) -> AsyncMock:
+    """Async-context-manager mock that yields a client whose .get returns a fixed response."""
+    mock_client = AsyncMock()
+    mock_client.get.return_value = _mock_response(status_code, json_data)
+    mock_client.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_client)
+    mock_client.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(return_value=False)
+    return mock_client
+
+
+def _patch_billing_client(mock_http: AsyncMock):
+    """Stub get_billing_client → fake BG client whose _get_client yields ``mock_http``."""
+    bg_client = BillingGatewayClient("http://billing.test")
+    return (
+        patch("app.services.billing_events.get_billing_client", return_value=bg_client),
+        patch.object(bg_client, "_get_client", return_value=mock_http),
+    )
+
+
+@pytest.mark.unit
+async def test_default_params_no_timestamps_sent():
+    """No timestamps → only page/per_page in params (bg applies its 24h default)."""
+    mock_http = _mock_http_client(200, {"events": [], "meta": {"total_count": 0}})
+
+    p1, p2 = _patch_billing_client(mock_http)
+    with p1, p2:
+        result = await list_customer_events("cust-1")
+
+    assert result == {"events": [], "meta": {"total_count": 0}}
+    mock_http.get.assert_called_once_with(
+        "http://billing.test/billing/customers/cust-1/events",
+        params={"page": 1, "per_page": 20},
+    )
+
+
+@pytest.mark.unit
+async def test_all_params_forwarded_verbatim():
+    payload = {"events": [{"lago_id": "x"}], "meta": {"total_count": 1}}
+    mock_http = _mock_http_client(200, payload)
+
+    p1, p2 = _patch_billing_client(mock_http)
+    with p1, p2:
+        result = await list_customer_events(
+            "cust-1",
+            timestamp_from="2026-04-21T00:00:00Z",
+            timestamp_to="2026-04-28T23:59:59Z",
+            page=3,
+            per_page=50,
+        )
+
+    assert result == payload
+    mock_http.get.assert_called_once_with(
+        "http://billing.test/billing/customers/cust-1/events",
+        params={
+            "page": 3,
+            "per_page": 50,
+            "timestamp_from": "2026-04-21T00:00:00Z",
+            "timestamp_to": "2026-04-28T23:59:59Z",
+        },
+    )
+
+
+@pytest.mark.unit
+async def test_propagates_http_error():
+    """4xx → _raise_for_status_with_body propagates; body is embedded in the error message."""
+    mock_http = _mock_http_client(422, {"detail": "bad timestamp"})
+
+    p1, p2 = _patch_billing_client(mock_http)
+    with p1, p2, pytest.raises(httpx.HTTPStatusError):
+        await list_customer_events("cust-1")

+'use client'
+
+import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'
+import { useState } from 'react'
+
+import { useToast } from '@/components/ui/Toast'
+import type { UserData } from '@/lib/api/admin-types'
+import { getAPI } from '@/lib/api/backend'
+
+import { handleMutationError } from '../lib/errorUtils'
+import { adminKeys } from '../lib/queryKeys'
+
+export type EventItem = {
+  lago_id: string
+  transaction_id: string
+  code: string
+  timestamp: string
+  external_subscription_id: string
+  created_at: string
+  properties: Record<string, unknown>
+}
+
+export type EventsMeta = {
+  current_page: number
+  next_page: number | null
+  prev_page: number | null
+  total_pages: number
+  total_count: number
+}
+
+type EventsResponse = { events: EventItem[]; meta: EventsMeta }
+
+const PER_PAGE = 50
+
+export type RangePreset = '24h' | '7d' | '30d' | 'custom'
+
+const PRESET_HOURS: Record<Exclude<RangePreset, 'custom'>, number> = {
+  '24h': 24,
+  '7d': 24 * 7,
+  '30d': 24 * 30,
+}
+
+// Lago expects ISO 8601 with `Z`, not `+00:00`. toISOString() gives ms precision —
+// strip the milliseconds for a friendlier query string.
+function isoFromHoursAgo(hours: number): string {
+  return new Date(Date.now() - hours * 3600_000).toISOString().replace(/\.\d+Z$/, 'Z')
+}
+
+const EMPTY_META: EventsMeta = {
+  current_page: 0,
+  next_page: null,
+  prev_page: null,
+  total_pages: 0,
+  total_count: 0,
+}
+
+export function useCustomerEvents() {
+  const { showToast } = useToast()
+  const [showModal, setShowModal] = useState(false)
+  const [target, setTarget] = useState<UserData | null>(null)
+  const [preset, setPreset] = useState<RangePreset>('24h')
+  const [customFrom, setCustomFrom] = useState<string>('')
+  const [customTo, setCustomTo] = useState<string>('')
+  const [page, setPage] = useState(1)
+
+  const resolved =
+    preset === 'custom'
+      ? {
+          timestamp_from: customFrom ? `${customFrom}T00:00:00Z` : null,
+          timestamp_to: customTo ? `${customTo}T23:59:59Z` : null,
+        }
+      : { timestamp_from: isoFromHoursAgo(PRESET_HOURS[preset]), timestamp_to: null }
+
+  const { data, isFetching, error, refetch } = useQuery({
+    queryKey: adminKeys.customerEvents(target?.uid ?? '', { ...resolved, page }),
+    queryFn: async () => {
+      try {
+        const qs = new URLSearchParams({
+          uid: target!.uid,
+          page: String(page),
+          per_page: String(PER_PAGE),
+        })
+        if (resolved.timestamp_from) qs.set('timestamp_from', resolved.timestamp_from)
+        if (resolved.timestamp_to) qs.set('timestamp_to', resolved.timestamp_to)
+        const result = await getAPI<EventsResponse>(`/api/admin/users/events?${qs.toString()}`)
+        if (!result.success) throw new Error(result.error || 'Failed to fetch events')
+        return result.data ?? { events: [], meta: EMPTY_META }
+      } catch (err: unknown) {
+        handleMutationError('获取事件失败', err, showToast)
+        throw err
+      }
+    },
+    enabled: !!target,
+    retry: false,
+  })
+
+  // Switching range invalidates the current page number — page 5 of "last 24h" is
+  // a different result set than page 5 of "last 30d", so reset to 1.
+  const selectPreset = (next: RangePreset) => {
+    setPreset(next)
+    setPage(1)
+  }
+
+  const open = (user: UserData) => {
+    setTarget(user)
+    setPreset('24h')
+    setCustomFrom('')
+    setCustomTo('')
+    setPage(1)
+    setShowModal(true)
+  }
+
+  const close = () => {
+    setShowModal(false)
+    setTarget(null)
+  }
+
+  return {
+    showModal,
+    target,
+    events: data?.events ?? [],
+    meta: data?.meta ?? EMPTY_META,
+    isFetching,
+    error,
+    preset,
+    customFrom,
+    customTo,
+    page,
+    perPage: PER_PAGE,
+    selectPreset,
+    setCustomFrom,
+    setCustomTo,
+    setPage,
+    refetch,
+    open,
+    close,
+  }
+}

+import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'
+
+import { createErrorResponse, proxyToBackend } from '@/lib/api/proxy'
+import { logger } from '@/lib/logger'
+
+/**
+ * GET /api/admin/users/events
+ *
+ * Admin endpoint listing a user's billing-gateway events (Lago passthrough).
+ * Registered in ADMIN_ROUTES + UID_VALIDATION_EXEMPT — the middleware enforces
+ * the admin role but skips UID-ownership validation since `uid` here is a filter,
+ * not the caller's identity.
+ */
+export async function GET(request: Request) {
+  try {
+    const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url)
+    const uid = searchParams.get('uid')
+    if (!uid) return createErrorResponse('uid is required', 400)
+
+    const page = Math.max(1, parseInt(searchParams.get('page') || '1', 10) || 1)
+    const perPage = Math.min(100, Math.max(1, parseInt(searchParams.get('per_page') || '20', 10) || 20))
+    const from = searchParams.get('timestamp_from')
+    const to = searchParams.get('timestamp_to')
+
+    const qs = new URLSearchParams({ page: String(page), per_page: String(perPage) })
+    if (from) qs.set('timestamp_from', from)
+    if (to) qs.set('timestamp_to', to)
+
+    const response = await proxyToBackend(
+      `/admin/users/${encodeURIComponent(uid)}/events?${qs.toString()}`,
+      undefined,
+      { method: 'GET', originalRequest: request },
+    )
+
+    const data = (await response.json()) as Record<string, unknown>
+    return NextResponse.json(data, { status: response.status })
+  } catch (error: unknown) {
+    logger.error('[API /admin/users/events] Error:', error)
+    return createErrorResponse(error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to fetch events', 500)
+  }
+}

+// @vitest-environment jsdom
+import { cleanup, fireEvent, render, screen } from '@testing-library/react'
+import { afterEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
+
+import { CustomerEventsModal } from '@/app/[locale]/admin/components/CustomerEventsModal'
+import type { EventItem, EventsMeta, RangePreset } from '@/app/[locale]/admin/hooks/useCustomerEvents'
+
+import { createMockUserData } from '../../../helpers/factories'
+
+vi.mock('@/lib/logger', () => ({
+  logger: { log: vi.fn(), info: vi.fn(), warn: vi.fn(), error: vi.fn() },
+}))
+
+const baseMeta: EventsMeta = {
+  current_page: 1,
+  next_page: 2,
+  prev_page: null,
+  total_pages: 5,
+  total_count: 250,
+}
+
+const sampleEvent: EventItem = {
+  lago_id: 'evt-1',
+  transaction_id: 'tx-1',
+  code: 'ecap_credits',
+  timestamp: '2026-04-28T12:38:32.513Z',
+  external_subscription_id: 'u1',
+  created_at: '2026-04-28T12:38:32Z',
+  properties: {
+    model: 'claude-haiku-4-5',
+    prompt_tokens: 23230,
+    completion_tokens: 8,
+    total_tokens: 23238,
+    response_cost: 0.0005042699999999999,
+  },
+}
+
+function renderModal(overrides: Partial<React.ComponentProps<typeof CustomerEventsModal>> = {}) {
+  const props: React.ComponentProps<typeof CustomerEventsModal> = {
+    showModal: true,
+    target: createMockUserData({ uid: 'u1' }),
+    events: [sampleEvent],
+    meta: baseMeta,
+    isFetching: false,
+    preset: '24h' as RangePreset,
+    customFrom: '',
+    customTo: '',
+    page: 1,
+    perPage: 50,
+    selectPreset: vi.fn(),
+    setCustomFrom: vi.fn(),
+    setCustomTo: vi.fn(),
+    setPage: vi.fn(),
+    refetch: vi.fn(),
+    close: vi.fn(),
+    ...overrides,
+  }
+  return { props, ...render(<CustomerEventsModal {...props} />) }
+}
+
+describe('CustomerEventsModal', () => {
+  afterEach(() => cleanup())
+
+  it('renders nothing when showModal is false', () => {
+    renderModal({ showModal: false })
+    expect(screen.queryByTestId('admin-customer-events-modal')).toBeNull()
+  })
+
+  it('renders the row data: code, model, tokens, cost', () => {
+    renderModal()
+    expect(screen.getByText('ecap_credits')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    expect(screen.getByText('claude-haiku-4-5')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    // Token columns use locale-formatted thousands; just assert the values appear.
+    expect(screen.getByText('23,230')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    expect(screen.getByText('8')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    expect(screen.getByText('23,238')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    // Cost rounded to 4 decimals.
+    expect(screen.getByText('$0.0005')).toBeInTheDocument()
+  })
+
+  it('renders empty-state when events is empty', () => {
+    renderModal({ events: [], meta: { ...baseMeta, total_count: 0 } })
+    expect(screen.getByText('该时间范围内无事件')).toBeInTheDocument()
+  })
+
+  it('renders loading spinner when isFetching', () => {
+    renderModal({ events: [], isFetching: true })
+    expect(screen.getByText('加载事件中...')).toBeInTheDocument()
+  })
+
+  it('calls selectPreset when a different range chip is clicked', () => {
+    const selectPreset = vi.fn()
+    renderModal({ selectPreset })
+    fireEvent.click(screen.getByTestId('admin-customer-events-range-7d'))
+    expect(selectPreset).toHaveBeenCalledWith('7d')
+  })
+
+  it('reveals date inputs and apply button only in custom preset', () => {
+    const { rerender, props } = renderModal()
+    // Default 24h: no custom inputs.
+    expect(screen.queryByTestId('admin-customer-events-custom-from')).toBeNull()
+    expect(screen.queryByTestId('admin-customer-events-apply')).toBeNull()
+
+    rerender(<CustomerEventsModal {...props} preset="custom" />)
+    expect(screen.getByTestId('admin-customer-events-custom-from')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    expect(screen.getByTestId('admin-customer-events-custom-to')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    expect(screen.getByTestId('admin-customer-events-apply')).toBeInTheDocument()
+  })
+
+  it('apply button resets page to 1 and refetches', () => {
+    const setPage = vi.fn()
+    const refetch = vi.fn()
+    renderModal({ preset: 'custom', customFrom: '2026-04-01', customTo: '2026-04-15', page: 4, setPage, refetch })
+
+    fireEvent.click(screen.getByTestId('admin-customer-events-apply'))
+    expect(setPage).toHaveBeenCalledWith(1)
+    expect(refetch).toHaveBeenCalled()
+  })
+
+  it('Prev disabled when prev_page is null; Next enabled when next_page is set', () => {
+    renderModal()
+    expect(screen.getByTestId('admin-customer-events-prev')).toBeDisabled()
+    expect(screen.getByTestId('admin-customer-events-next')).not.toBeDisabled()
+  })
+
+  it('Next disabled when next_page is null', () => {
+    renderModal({ meta: { ...baseMeta, next_page: null, prev_page: 1, current_page: 5 } })
+    expect(screen.getByTestId('admin-customer-events-next')).toBeDisabled()
+    expect(screen.getByTestId('admin-customer-events-prev')).not.toBeDisabled()
+  })
+
+  it('Next click increments page', () => {
+    const setPage = vi.fn()
+    renderModal({ page: 2, setPage })
+    fireEvent.click(screen.getByTestId('admin-customer-events-next'))
+    expect(setPage).toHaveBeenCalledWith(3)
+  })
+
+  it('close button invokes close()', () => {
+    const close = vi.fn()
+    renderModal({ close })
+    fireEvent.click(screen.getByTestId('admin-customer-events-close-button'))
+    expect(close).toHaveBeenCalled()
+  })
+
+  it('handles missing properties gracefully (renders dashes)', () => {
+    const ev: EventItem = { ...sampleEvent, properties: {} }
+    renderModal({ events: [ev], meta: { ...baseMeta, total_count: 1, total_pages: 1, next_page: null } })
+    // Model + tokens + cost all fall back to dashes.
+    const dashCells = screen.getAllByText('—')
+    expect(dashCells.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5)
+  })
+})

+// @vitest-environment jsdom
+import { act, renderHook, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react'
+import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
+
+import { createMockUserData } from '../../../helpers/factories'
+import { createQueryWrapper, createTestQueryClient } from '../../../helpers/queryWrapper'
+
+const mockShowToast = vi.fn()
+vi.mock('@/components/ui/Toast', () => ({
+  useToast: () => ({ showToast: mockShowToast }),
+}))
+
+vi.mock('@/lib/logger', () => ({
+  logger: { log: vi.fn(), info: vi.fn(), warn: vi.fn(), error: vi.fn() },
+}))
+
+const mockGetAPI = vi.fn()
+vi.mock('@/lib/api/backend', () => ({
+  getAPI: (...args: unknown[]) => mockGetAPI(...args),
+}))
+
+// Sample event matching staging bg response shape.
+const sampleEvent = {
+  lago_id: 'evt-1',
+  transaction_id: 'tx-1',
+  code: 'ecap_credits',
+  timestamp: '2026-04-28T12:38:32.513Z',
+  external_subscription_id: 'u1',
+  created_at: '2026-04-28T12:38:32Z',
+  properties: {
+    model: 'claude-haiku-4-5',
+    prompt_tokens: 100,
+    completion_tokens: 8,
+    total_tokens: 108,
+    response_cost: 0.0005,
+  },
+}
+
+const sampleMeta = { current_page: 1, next_page: 2, prev_page: null, total_pages: 5, total_count: 250 }
+
+describe('useCustomerEvents', () => {
+  let queryClient: ReturnType<typeof createTestQueryClient>
+  // Frozen "now" so preset windows produce stable timestamps. Using spyOn(Date, 'now')
+  // rather than vi.useFakeTimers() because the project's CLAUDE.md notes that fake
+  // timers freeze React Query's internal waitFor poll → tests hang.
+  const FIXED_NOW = Date.UTC(2026, 3, 28, 12, 0, 0) // 2026-04-28T12:00:00Z
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    vi.clearAllMocks()
+    vi.spyOn(Date, 'now').mockReturnValue(FIXED_NOW)
+    queryClient = createTestQueryClient()
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    queryClient.clear()
+    vi.restoreAllMocks()
+  })
+
+  async function renderEventsHook() {
+    const mod = await import('@/app/[locale]/admin/hooks/useCustomerEvents')
+    return renderHook(() => mod.useCustomerEvents(), { wrapper: createQueryWrapper(queryClient) })
+  }
+
+  it('does not fetch until modal is opened', async () => {
+    const { result } = await renderEventsHook()
+    expect(result.current.showModal).toBe(false)
+    expect(result.current.target).toBeNull()
+    expect(mockGetAPI).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
+  })
+
+  it('fetches with the 24h preset by default and forwards timestamp_from', async () => {
+    mockGetAPI.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { events: [sampleEvent], meta: sampleMeta } })
+    const { result } = await renderEventsHook()
+
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.open(createMockUserData({ uid: 'u1' }))
+    })
+
+    await waitFor(() => {
+      expect(result.current.events).toEqual([sampleEvent])
+    })
+
+    // Default preset is 24h → timestamp_from = FIXED_NOW − 24h = 2026-04-27T12:00:00Z
+    const url = mockGetAPI.mock.calls[0][0] as string
+    expect(url).toContain('uid=u1')
+    expect(url).toContain('per_page=50')
+    expect(url).toContain('page=1')
+    expect(url).toContain('timestamp_from=2026-04-27T12%3A00%3A00Z')
+    expect(url).not.toContain('timestamp_to=')
+  })
+
+  it('switching to 7d preset refetches with the wider window and resets page to 1', async () => {
+    mockGetAPI.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { events: [], meta: sampleMeta } })
+    const { result } = await renderEventsHook()
+
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.open(createMockUserData({ uid: 'u1' }))
+    })
+    await waitFor(() => expect(mockGetAPI).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1))
+
+    // Advance to page 3, then switch preset — page should reset to 1.
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.setPage(3)
+    })
+    await waitFor(() => expect(mockGetAPI).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2))
+
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.selectPreset('7d')
+    })
+
+    await waitFor(() => {
+      expect(mockGetAPI).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(3)
+      expect(result.current.page).toBe(1)
+    })
+
+    const lastUrl = mockGetAPI.mock.calls[2][0] as string
+    // 7d preset → timestamp_from = FIXED_NOW − 7d = 2026-04-21T12:00:00Z
+    expect(lastUrl).toContain('timestamp_from=2026-04-21T12%3A00%3A00Z')
+    expect(lastUrl).toContain('page=1')
+  })
+
+  it('custom preset with both dates serializes start-of-day and end-of-day UTC', async () => {
+    mockGetAPI.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { events: [], meta: sampleMeta } })
+    const { result } = await renderEventsHook()
+
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.open(createMockUserData({ uid: 'u1' }))
+    })
+    await waitFor(() => expect(mockGetAPI).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1))
+
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.selectPreset('custom')
+      result.current.setCustomFrom('2026-04-01')
+      result.current.setCustomTo('2026-04-15')
+    })
+    await waitFor(() => expect(mockGetAPI).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2))
+
+    const url = mockGetAPI.mock.calls[mockGetAPI.mock.calls.length - 1][0] as string
+    expect(url).toContain('timestamp_from=2026-04-01T00%3A00%3A00Z')
+    expect(url).toContain('timestamp_to=2026-04-15T23%3A59%3A59Z')
+  })
+
+  it('shows toast on fetch error', async () => {
+    mockGetAPI.mockResolvedValue({ success: false, error: 'Server error' })
+    const { result } = await renderEventsHook()
+
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.open(createMockUserData({ uid: 'u1' }))
+    })
+
+    await waitFor(() => {
+      expect(mockShowToast).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.stringContaining('获取事件失败'), 'error')
+    })
+  })
+
+  it('open() resets state (preset, custom dates, page)', async () => {
+    mockGetAPI.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { events: [], meta: sampleMeta } })
+    const { result } = await renderEventsHook()
+
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.open(createMockUserData({ uid: 'u1' }))
+    })
+    await waitFor(() => expect(mockGetAPI).toHaveBeenCalled())
+
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.selectPreset('custom')
+      result.current.setCustomFrom('2026-01-01')
+      result.current.setCustomTo('2026-01-31')
+      result.current.setPage(7)
+    })
+
+    // Open with a different user should reset everything.
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.open(createMockUserData({ uid: 'u2' }))
+    })
+
+    expect(result.current.preset).toBe('24h')
+    expect(result.current.customFrom).toBe('')
+    expect(result.current.customTo).toBe('')
+    expect(result.current.page).toBe(1)
+  })
+
+  it('close() hides modal and clears target', async () => {
+    mockGetAPI.mockResolvedValue({ success: true, data: { events: [], meta: sampleMeta } })
+    const { result } = await renderEventsHook()
+
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.open(createMockUserData({ uid: 'u1' }))
+    })
+    await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.showModal).toBe(true))
+
+    act(() => {
+      result.current.close()
+    })
+
+    expect(result.current.showModal).toBe(false)
+    expect(result.current.target).toBeNull()
+  })
+})
